description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about multiplication and division to solve problems with fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that involve adding and subtracting fractions, even with different denominators, and can estimate answers to check if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different denominators by finding equivalent fractions with the same denominator.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use equivalent fractions to help add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties.
description_plain: Your child can sort and organize 2D shapes into groups based on their properties.

subject: Math
description: Understand that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all subcategories of that category.
description_plain: Your child understands that if a shape has certain features, all shapes in its subgroups have those features too.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can sort and group flat shapes based on their features, like number of sides or angles.

subject: Math
description: Represent real world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane, and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a graph to show real-life or math problems and explain what the points mean.

subject: Math
description: Use a pair of perpendicular number lines, called axes, to define a coordinate system, with the intersection of the lines (the origin) arranged to coincide with the 0 on each line and a given point in the plane located by using an ordered pair of numbers, called its coordinates. Understand that the first number indicates how far to travel from the origin in the direction of one axis, and the second number indicates how far to travel in the direction of the second axis, with the convention that the names of the two axes and the coordinates correspond (e.g., x-axis and x-coordinate, y-axis and y-coordinate).
description_plain: Your child can use two crossing number lines to find and name locations on a grid using pairs of numbers.

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use graphs to solve real-world and math problems by plotting points.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as additive. Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.
description_plain: Your child understands that you can add the volumes of two separate box-shaped objects to find the total volume.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V = l × w × h and V = b × h for rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of a box by multiplying its length, width, and height.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths by packing it with unit cubes, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths, equivalently by multiplying the height by the area of the base. Represent threefold whole-number products as volumes, e.g., to represent the associative property of multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a box by filling it with cubes and by multiplying its side lengths, and can show that both ways give the same answer.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and addition and solve real world and mathematical problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child can use multiplication and addition to solve real-life and math problems about volume.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft, and improvised units.
description_plain: Your child can measure how much space a solid object takes up by counting cubes of different sizes.

subject: Math
description: A solid figure which can be packed without gaps or overlaps using n unit cubes is said to have a volume of n cubic units.
description_plain: Your child understands that if a solid can be filled with n cubes without gaps, its volume is n cubic units.

subject: Math
description: A cube with side length 1 unit, called a "unit cube," is said to have "one cubic unit" of volume, and can be used to measure volume.
description_plain: Your child knows that a cube with sides of 1 unit is called a unit cube and has a volume of one cubic unit.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of solid figures and understand concepts of volume measurement.
description_plain: Your child understands that solid objects have volume and knows how to measure it.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child understands what volume is and can use multiplication and addition to figure it out.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Use operations on fractions for this grade to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements using fractions and solve problems using the data.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Your child can show and explain data in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Convert among different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system (e.g., convert 5 cm to 0.05 m), and use these conversions in solving multi-step, real world problems.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another within the same system and use these conversions to solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system.
description_plain: Your child can convert measurements to different units within the same system.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals up to the hundredths place and explain how they solved the problems.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide large numbers by smaller numbers and show their work using different methods like drawings or equations.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and accurately multiply large whole numbers using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with multi-digit whole numbers and with decimals to hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can work with large numbers and decimals, using all four operations (add, subtract, multiply, divide).

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round decimals to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round decimals to any place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals up to the thousandths place and use symbols like >, <, or = to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form, e.g., 347.392 = 3 × 100 + 4 × 10 + 7 × 1 + 3 × (1/10) + 9 × (1/100) + 2 × (1/1000).
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals up to the thousandths place in numbers, words, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and compare decimals up to the thousandths place.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10, and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.
description_plain: Your child can explain what happens to the number of zeros or the decimal point when multiplying or dividing by powers of 10.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right and 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.
description_plain: Your child understands that each place in a number is ten times bigger than the place to its right and one-tenth the place to its left.

subject: Math
description: Understand the place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how place value works in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules. Identify apparent relationships between corresponding terms. Form ordered pairs consisting of corresponding terms from the two patterns, and graph the ordered pairs on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can make two number patterns using different rules, find how the patterns are related, and graph the results.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns and relationships.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain patterns and relationships in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that record calculations with numbers, and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions and explain what they mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Use parentheses, brackets, or braces in numerical expressions, and evaluate expressions with these symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use parentheses, brackets, or braces in math problems and solve them correctly.

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write and understand math expressions using numbers and symbols.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns in math and use them to solve problems more easily.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can spot helpful structures or patterns in math problems.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child pays attention to details and uses math terms and numbers correctly.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child chooses the best tools, like rulers or calculators, to solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child can use math to solve real-life problems and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and understand or question the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and words to explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child will try to understand math problems and keep working until they solve them.

subject: Math
description: Compose simple shapes to form larger shapes.
description_plain: Your child can put simple shapes together to make bigger shapes.

subject: Math
description: Model shapes in the world by building shapes from components (e.g., sticks and clay balls) and drawing shapes.
description_plain: Your child can build and draw shapes using things like sticks, clay, or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and compare two- and three-dimensional shapes, in different sizes and orientations, using informal language to describe their similarities, differences, parts (e.g., number of sides and vertices/"corners") and other attributes (e.g., having sides of equal length).
description_plain: Your child can look at different shapes and talk about how they are alike or different, using words like sides and corners.

subject: Math
description: Analyze, compare, create, and compose shapes.
description_plain: Your child can look at, compare, make, and put together different shapes.

subject: Math
description: Identify shapes as two-dimensional (lying in a plane, "flat") or three-dimensional ("solid").
description_plain: Your child can tell if a shape is flat (like a piece of paper) or solid (like a ball or box).

subject: Math
description: Correctly name shapes regardless of their orientations or overall size.
description_plain: Your child can correctly name shapes no matter how they are turned or how big they are.

subject: Math
description: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes, and describe the relative positions of these objects using terms such as above, below, beside, in front of, behind, and next to.
description_plain: Your child can use shape names to describe things around them and say where things are, like above, below, or next to.

subject: Math
description: Identify and describe shapes (squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres).
description_plain: Your child can find and talk about shapes like squares, circles, triangles, rectangles, hexagons, cubes, cones, cylinders, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Classify objects into given categories; count the numbers of objects in each category and sort the categories by count.
description_plain: Your child can sort objects into groups, count how many are in each group, and put the groups in order by number.

subject: Math
description: Classify objects and count the number of objects in each category.
description_plain: Your child can sort objects into groups and count how many are in each group.

subject: Math
description: Directly compare two objects with a measurable attribute in common, to see which object has "more of"/"less of" the attribute, and describe the difference.
description_plain: Your child can compare two things, like which is longer or heavier, and tell how they are different.

subject: Math
description: Describe measurable attributes of objects, such as length or weight. Describe several measurable attributes of a single object.
description_plain: Your child can talk about things you can measure, like how long or heavy something is, and can name more than one way to measure an object.

subject: Math
description: Describe and compare measurable attributes.
description_plain: Your child can describe and compare things you can measure, like size or weight.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose numbers from 11 to 19 into ten ones and some further ones, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each composition or decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 18 = 10 + 8); understand that these numbers are composed of ten ones and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers from 11 to 19 into ten and some more ones, using objects, drawings, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Work with numbers 11—19 to gain foundations for place value.
description_plain: Your child will work with numbers 11 to 19 to understand how place value works.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract within 5.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and correctly add and subtract numbers up to 5.

subject: Math
description: For any number from 1 to 9, find the number that makes 10 when added to the given number, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record the answer with a drawing or equation.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what number needs to be added to another number (from 1 to 9) to make 10, using objects or drawings.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers less than or equal to 10 into pairs in more than one way, e.g., by using objects or drawings, and record each decomposition by a drawing or equation (e.g., 5 = 2 + 3 and 5 = 4 + 1).
description_plain: Your child can break numbers up to 10 into two groups in different ways and show this with objects, drawings, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems, and add and subtract within 10, e.g., by using objects or drawings to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple addition and subtraction story problems using objects or drawings for numbers up to 10.

subject: Math
description: Represent addition and subtraction with objects, fingers, mental images, drawings, sounds (e.g., claps), acting out situations, verbal explanations, expressions, or equations.
description_plain: Your child can show addition and subtraction using objects, fingers, drawings, sounds, acting, or equations.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition as putting together and adding to, and understand subtraction as taking apart and taking from.
description_plain: Your child understands that adding means putting things together and subtracting means taking things away.

subject: Math
description: Compare two numbers between 1 and 10 presented as written numerals.
description_plain: Your child can look at two written numbers between 1 and 10 and say which is bigger or if they are the same.

subject: Math
description: Identify whether the number of objects in one group is greater than, less than, or equal to the number of objects in another group, e.g., by using matching and counting strategies.
description_plain: Your child can tell if one group has more, fewer, or the same number of objects as another group by counting or matching.

subject: Math
description: Compare numbers.
description_plain: Your child can compare numbers to see which is bigger, smaller, or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Count to answer "how many?" questions about as many as 20 things arranged in a line, a rectangular array, or a circle, or as many as 10 things in a scattered configuration; given a number from 1—20, count out that many objects.
description_plain: Your child can count up to 20 objects in a line, array, circle, or up to 10 scattered objects, and can count out a specific number of objects when asked.

subject: Math
description: Understand that each successive number name refers to a quantity that is one larger.
description_plain: Your child understands that each new number means one more than the last when counting.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the last number name said tells the number of objects counted. The number of objects is the same regardless of their arrangement or the order in which they were counted.
description_plain: Your child knows that the last number they say when counting tells how many objects there are, no matter how the objects are arranged.

subject: Math
description: When counting objects, say the number names in the standard order, pairing each object with one and only one number name and each number name with one and only one object.
description_plain: Your child can count objects one by one, making sure each object gets only one number.

subject: Math
description: Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities; connect counting to cardinality.
description_plain: Your child understands how counting matches up with the number of objects and can connect numbers to amounts.

subject: Math
description: Count to tell the number of objects.
description_plain: Your child can count objects and tell how many there are.

subject: Math
description: Write numbers from 0 to 20. Represent a number of objects with a written numeral 0-20 (with 0 representing a count of no objects).
description_plain: Your child can write numbers from 0 to 20 and use these numbers to show how many objects there are.

subject: Math
description: Count forward beginning from a given number within the known sequence (instead of having to begin at 1).
description_plain: Your child can start counting from any number they know, not just from 1.

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.
description_plain: Your child can count to 100 by ones and by tens.

subject: Math
description: Know number names and the count sequence.
description_plain: Your child knows the names and order of numbers and can count in sequence.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns in math and use them to help solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can spot helpful features or patterns in math problems to make solving them easier.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child pays attention to being clear and accurate when doing math.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like blocks or a ruler, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child can use math to solve real-life problems, like counting objects or measuring things.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to others’ ideas to see if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child understands how to think about numbers and problems in different ways, using both pictures and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child will keep trying different ways to solve a math problem until they find an answer.

subject: Math
description: Describe qualitatively the functional relationship between two quantities by analyzing a graph (e.g., where the function is increasing or decreasing, linear or nonlinear). Sketch a graph that exhibits the qualitative features of a function that has been described verbally.
description_plain: Your child can look at a graph and describe how two things are related, like if one goes up or down, and can draw a graph based on a description.

subject: Math
description: Construct a function to model a linear relationship between two quantities. Determine the rate of change and initial value of the function from a description of a relationship or from two (x, y) values, including reading these from a table or from a graph. Interpret the rate of change and initial value of a linear function in terms of the situation it models, and in terms of its graph or a table of values.
description_plain: Your child can create a math rule (function) to show how two things are connected, find out how fast one changes compared to the other, and explain what these numbers mean in a real situation.

subject: Math
description: Use functions to model relationships between quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules (functions) to show how two things are related.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the equation y = mx + b as defining a linear function, whose graph is a straight line; give examples of functions that are not linear.
description_plain: Your child understands that equations like y = mx + b make straight lines on a graph, and can give examples of rules that do not make straight lines.

subject: Math
description: Compare properties of two functions each represented in a different way (algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, or by verbal descriptions).
description_plain: Your child can compare two math rules (functions) even if they are shown in different ways, like in a table, graph, equation, or words.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a function is a rule that assigns to each input exactly one output. The graph of a function is the set of ordered pairs consisting of an input and the corresponding output.
description_plain: Your child understands that a function is a rule where each input has only one output, and that its graph shows all the matching pairs.

subject: Math
description: Define, evaluate, and compare functions.
description_plain: Your child can define, use, and compare different math rules (functions).

subject: Math
description: Understand that patterns of association can also be seen in bivariate categorical data by displaying frequencies and relative frequencies in a two-way table. Construct and interpret a two-way table summarizing data on two categorical variables collected from the same subjects. Use relative frequencies calculated for rows or columns to describe possible association between the two variables.
description_plain: Your child can make and read tables that show how two groups of information are related, and use these tables to look for patterns.

subject: Math
description: Use the equation of a linear model to solve problems in the context of bivariate measurement data, interpreting the slope and intercept.
description_plain: Your child can use a line equation to solve problems with two sets of measurements, and can explain what the slope and starting point mean.

subject: Math
description: Know that straight lines are widely used to model relationships between two quantitative variables. For scatter plots that suggest a linear association, informally fit a straight line, and informally assess the model fit by judging the closeness of the data points to the line.
description_plain: Your child knows that straight lines can show how two things are related, can draw a line that fits a set of points, and can judge how well the line matches the data.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association between two quantities. Describe patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative association, linear association, and nonlinear association.
description_plain: Your child can make and understand scatter plots to look for patterns between two things, and can describe those patterns.

subject: Math
description: Investigate patterns of association in bivariate data.
description_plain: Your child can find and describe patterns between two sets of data.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems leading to two linear equations in two variables.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems that involve finding answers to two equations with two variables.

subject: Math
description: Solve systems of two linear equations in two variables algebraically, and estimate solutions by graphing the equations. Solve simple cases by inspection.
description_plain: Your child can solve two equations with two variables using math steps or by graphing, and can spot simple answers by looking.

subject: Math
description: Understand that solutions to a system of two linear equations in two variables correspond to points of intersection of their graphs, because points of intersection satisfy both equations simultaneously.
description_plain: Your child understands that the answer to two equations is where their lines cross on a graph, because that point works for both equations.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve pairs of simultaneous linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can analyze and solve problems with two equations at the same time.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations with rational number coefficients, including equations whose solutions require expanding expressions using the distributive property and collecting like terms.
description_plain: Your child can solve equations with fractions or decimals, even when they need to use steps like distributing and combining like terms.

subject: Math
description: Give examples of linear equations in one variable with one solution, infinitely many solutions, or no solutions. Show which of these possibilities is the case by successively transforming the given equation into simpler forms, until an equivalent equation of the form x = a, a = a, or a = b results (where a and b are different numbers).
description_plain: Your child can give examples of equations with one answer, many answers, or no answer, and show why by simplifying the equation.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can solve equations with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve linear equations and pairs of simultaneous linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve equations with one variable or two equations with two variables.

subject: Math
description: Use similar triangles to explain why the slope m is the same between any two distinct points on a non-vertical line in the coordinate plane; derive the equation y = mx for a line through the origin and the equation y = mx + b for a line intercepting the vertical axis at b.
description_plain: Your child can use triangles to explain why the slope of a straight line is always the same, and can write equations for lines.

subject: Math
description: Graph proportional relationships, interpreting the unit rate as the slope of the graph. Compare two different proportional relationships represented in different ways.
description_plain: Your child can draw graphs to show proportional relationships, understand that the slope is the unit rate, and compare different relationships.

subject: Math
description: Understand the connections between proportional relationships, lines, and linear equations.
description_plain: Your child understands how proportional relationships, straight lines, and equations are connected.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with numbers expressed in scientific notation, including problems where both decimal and scientific notation are used. Use scientific notation and choose units of appropriate size for measurements of very large or very small quantities (e.g., use millimeters per year for seafloor spreading). Interpret scientific notation that has been generated by technology.
description_plain: Your child can do math with numbers written in scientific notation, use the right units for big or small measurements, and understand scientific notation from a calculator or computer.

subject: Math
description: Use numbers expressed in the form of a single digit times an integer power of 10 to estimate very large or very small quantities, and to express how many times as much one is than the other.
description_plain: Your child can use numbers like 3 × 10⁵ to estimate very big or very small amounts, and compare sizes.

subject: Math
description: Use square root and cube root symbols to represent solutions to equations of the form x² = p and x³ = p, where p is a positive rational number. Evaluate square roots of small perfect squares and cube roots of small perfect cubes. Know that √2 is irrational.
description_plain: Your child can use square root and cube root symbols to solve equations, find roots of small numbers, and knows that the square root of 2 is not a simple fraction.

subject: Math
description: Know and apply the properties of integer exponents to generate equivalent numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses the rules for exponents to write numbers in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Work with radicals and integer exponents.
description_plain: Your child can work with roots and exponents in math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use rational approximations of irrational numbers to compare the size of irrational numbers, locate them approximately on a number line diagram, and estimate the value of expressions (e.g., π²).
description_plain: Your child can use fractions and decimals to estimate and compare numbers that can't be written as simple fractions, and can find about where they are on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Know that numbers that are not rational are called irrational. Understand informally that every number has a decimal expansion; for rational numbers show that the decimal expansion repeats eventually, and convert a decimal expansion which repeats eventually into a rational number.
description_plain: Your child knows that some numbers can't be written as fractions, understands that every number has a decimal form, and can turn repeating decimals into fractions.

subject: Math
description: Know that there are numbers that are not rational, and approximate them by rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child knows that some numbers are not fractions and can estimate them using fractions or decimals.

subject: Math
description: Know the formulas for the volumes of cones, cylinders, and spheres and use them to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child knows the formulas for finding the volume of cones, cylinders, and spheres, and can use them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about the volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points in a coordinate system.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles in real-world and mathematical problems in two and three dimensions.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find missing side lengths in right triangles, even in real-life or 3D problems.

subject: Math
description: Explain a proof of the Pythagorean Theorem and its converse.
description_plain: Your child can explain why the Pythagorean Theorem works and what its opposite (converse) means.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply the Pythagorean Theorem.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use the Pythagorean Theorem to solve problems about right triangles.

subject: Math
description: Use informal arguments to establish facts about the angle sum and exterior angle of triangles, about the angles created when parallel lines are cut by a transversal, and the angle-angle criterion for similarity of triangles.
description_plain: Your child can use simple explanations to show why the angles in a triangle add up to 180 degrees, how angles work when lines are parallel and crossed by another line, and how to tell if triangles are similar by comparing their angles.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is similar to another if the second can be obtained from the first by a sequence of rotations, reflections, translations, and dilations; given two similar two-dimensional figures, describe a sequence that exhibits the similarity between them.
description_plain: Your child understands that one shape is similar to another if you can turn, flip, slide, or resize it to match, and can explain how to do this.

subject: Math
description: Describe the effect of dilations, translations, rotations, and reflections on two-dimensional figures using coordinates.
description_plain: Your child can describe what happens to a shape's position or size on a grid when it is moved, turned, flipped, or resized.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is congruent to another if the second can be obtained from the first by a sequence of rotations, reflections, and translations; given two congruent figures, describe a sequence that exhibits the congruence between them.
description_plain: Your child understands that two shapes are congruent if you can turn, flip, or slide one to exactly match the other, and can explain how to do this.

subject: Math
description: Parallel lines are taken to parallel lines.
description_plain: Your child understands that when you move parallel lines using certain moves, they stay parallel.

subject: Math
description: Angles are taken to angles of the same measure.
description_plain: Your child understands that when you move shapes using certain moves, the angles stay the same size.

subject: Math
description: Lines are taken to lines, and line segments to line segments of the same length.
description_plain: Your child understands that when you move shapes using certain moves, lines and line segments stay the same length.

subject: Math
description: Verify experimentally the properties of rotations, reflections, and translations:
description_plain: Your child can use hands-on activities to show what happens to shapes when they are turned, flipped, or slid.

subject: Math
description: Understand congruence and similarity using physical models, transparencies, or geometry software.
description_plain: Your child understands what it means for shapes to be the same (congruent) or similar by using models, clear sheets, or computer programs.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child will notice patterns in their work and use those patterns to solve problems more easily.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child will look for ways to break down math problems into smaller, familiar parts.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Your child will use math words and numbers carefully and accurately.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child will choose the best tools, like rulers or calculators, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Your child will use math to solve real-life problems and explain their thinking.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child will explain their math ideas clearly and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child will think about numbers and problems in different ways, using both pictures and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child will keep working on math problems, even when they are challenging, and try different strategies to solve them.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.
description_plain: Your child will look at test results from two objects made to solve the same problem and compare how well each one works.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.
description_plain: Your child will draw a picture or make a simple model to show how the shape of something helps it do its job.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation people want to change to define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.
description_plain: Your child will ask questions, observe, and gather information to figure out a simple problem that can be solved by making or improving something.

subject: Science
description: Obtain information to identify where fresh water is found on Earth, including the Great Lakes and Great Lakes Basin.
description_plain: Your child will find out where fresh water is found on Earth, including the Great Lakes and their surrounding area.

subject: Science
description: Obtain information to identify where water is found on Earth and that it can be solid or liquid.
description_plain: Your child will find out where water is found on Earth and learn that water can be either solid (like ice) or liquid.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to represent the state of Michigan and the Great Lakes, or a more local land area and water body.
description_plain: Your child will make a model to show the state of Michigan and the Great Lakes, or a local area with land and water.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to represent the shapes and kinds of land and bodies of water in an area.
description_plain: Your child will make a model to show the shapes and types of land and water in a certain area.

subject: Science
description: Compare multiple solutions designed to slow or prevent wind or water from changing the shape of the land.
description_plain: Your child will compare different ways to stop wind or water from changing the shape of the land.

subject: Science
description: Use information from several sources to provide evidence that Earth events can occur quickly or slowly.
description_plain: Your child will use information from different sources to show that some changes on Earth happen quickly and some happen slowly.

subject: Science
description: Make observations of plants and animals to compare the diversity of life in different habitats.
description_plain: Your child will observe plants and animals to compare the variety of living things in different places.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple model that mimics the function of an animal in dispersing seeds or pollinating plants.
description_plain: Your child will make a simple model to show how an animal can help spread seeds or pollinate plants.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to determine if plants need sunlight and water to grow.
description_plain: Your child will plan and carry out an experiment to find out if plants need sunlight and water to grow.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that some changes caused by heating or cooling can be reversed and some cannot.
description_plain: Your child will use evidence to explain that some changes from heating or cooling can be undone, but others cannot.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account of how an object made of a small set of pieces can be disassembled and made into a new object.
description_plain: Your child will observe how an object made of small pieces can be taken apart and used to make something new.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data obtained from testing different materials to determine which materials have the properties that are best suited for an intended purpose.
description_plain: Your child will look at test results to decide which materials are best for a certain use based on their properties.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to describe and classify different kinds of materials by their observable properties.
description_plain: Your child will plan and do an experiment to sort and describe different materials by how they look and feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being (e.g., quizzed, whined, stammered) and that are basic to a particular topic (e.g., wildlife, conservation, and endangered when discussing animal preservation).
description_plain: Your child will learn and use new words that are important for school subjects and topics, including words that show actions, feelings, or describe things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child will show they understand words by connecting them to their opposites and to words with similar meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child will recognize and explain the meanings of common sayings and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors (e.g., as pretty as a picture) in context.
description_plain: Your child will explain what simple comparisons like similes and metaphors mean in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child will understand and use figurative language, word relationships, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child will use dictionaries or other reference tools, in print or online, to find out how to say and understand the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., telegraph, photograph, autograph).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., definitions, examples, or restatements in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the rest of the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies, like looking at word parts or using context clues.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion).
description_plain: Your child understands when to use formal English (like for presentations) and when it's okay to speak more casually (like with friends).

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation marks to make their writing more interesting or clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child can pick the right words and phrases to clearly share their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly for their grade and knows how to look up words if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma before words like 'and,' 'but,' or 'or' when joining two sentences together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing what someone says or quoting from a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct capitalization.
description_plain: Your child uses capital letters correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two; there, their).
description_plain: Your child can use commonly confused words (like 'to,' 'too,' and 'two') correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child can write complete sentences and fix sentence fragments or run-on sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use prepositional phrases (like 'under the table' or 'after school') in their sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns (e.g., a small red bag rather than a red small bag).
description_plain: Your child can put describing words (adjectives) in the right order in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries (e.g., can, may, must) to convey various conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use helping words like 'can,' 'may,' or 'must' to show different possibilities or needs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive (e.g., I was walking; I am walking; I will be walking) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use the correct form of verbs to show something happening now, in the past, or in the future (like 'I am walking,' 'I was walking,' 'I will be walking').

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relative pronouns (who, whose, whom, which, that) and relative adverbs (where, when, why).
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'who,' 'whose,' 'which,' and 'when' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses proper grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a variety of words for school subjects and everyday life, and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words are related, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what unfamiliar words mean by using clues from the sentence, breaking down the word, or looking it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use language differently depending on the situation, choose words for meaning or style, and better understand what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses proper grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child understands when to use formal English and when it's okay to speak more casually, and can use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add audio or visual elements to presentations to help explain their main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information, tell a story, or describe an experience with good details and facts, speaking at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can point out the reasons and evidence a speaker uses to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate information they've heard or seen in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can review the main ideas from a discussion and explain their own thoughts about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to better understand a topic and add helpful comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow the rules for group discussions and do their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or learned about the topic, and uses that knowledge to join in the conversation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, build on others' ideas, and share their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visual aids to help explain information and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence in a way that is organized and easy for others to follow, using the right style for the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and how well they use evidence to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use information that is shown in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child will join in different kinds of group discussions, listen to others, share their own ideas, and explain their thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write often, sometimes working on longer projects and sometimes finishing shorter pieces, for different subjects and purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to informational texts (e.g., "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text").
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in nonfiction texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text [e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions].").
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, settings, or events in stories using specific details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction texts to support their ideas and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes and categorize information, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find important information, take notes, organize what they learn, and list where they got their information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of one page in a single sitting.
description_plain: With some help, your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others, and can type at least one page at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense for the story or experience they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses specific words and details that help readers imagine the experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to show the order of events in their stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses conversations and descriptions to show what happens and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situationand introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts their story by introducing the situation and characters, and organizes the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events, using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (e.g., another, for example, also, because).
description_plain: Your child connects ideas in their writing using words like 'also,' 'because,' or 'for example.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces a topic, groups related information together, and uses things like headings or pictures to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic and share ideas and information clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that matches the opinion they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., for instance, in order to, in addition).
description_plain: Your child connects their opinion and reasons using words like 'for instance' or 'in addition.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons for their opinion and backs them up with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces the topic, states their opinion, and organizes ideas to support their view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic, giving reasons and information to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes working on longer projects and sometimes finishing shorter pieces, for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction texts to support their ideas and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find important information from different sources, check if it is trustworthy, and use it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects to answer questions and show what they have learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events, using good details and a clear order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic and share ideas and information clearly and accurately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to support their opinions or claims, using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the text to figure out or double-check words and their meanings, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books or passages with a clear purpose and understands what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use their knowledge of letter sounds, word parts, and word patterns to read new long words, both in and out of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use fourth-grade phonics and word skills to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 4—5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles at a fourth- or fifth-grade level, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can use information from two different texts about the same topic to write or talk knowledgeably about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support their points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in charts, graphs, diagrams, or other visuals, and tell how it helps them understand the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand account (someone who was there) and a secondhand account (someone who heard about it) of the same event, and describe how they are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a nonfiction text is organized, such as by time order, comparing things, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words or phrases in fourth-grade nonfiction texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain what happened and why in history, science, or technical texts, using details from the reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a nonfiction text, explain how details support it, and summarize what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says directly and to make logical guesses about what is not directly stated.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in the grades 4—5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at a fourth- or fifth-grade level, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast how different stories, myths, or traditional tales from various cultures handle similar themes or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect what happens in a story or play to a visual or spoken version, and point out where each version matches the original text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast the point of view in different stories, including knowing the difference between first-person and third-person narration.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems (e.g., verse, rhythm, meter) and drama (e.g., casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between poems, plays, and regular stories, and use the right terms when talking or writing about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including those that allude to significant characters found in mythology (e.g., Herculean).
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including those that refer to famous characters from myths.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, setting, or event in detail, using specific examples from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem from details in the text and summarize what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a story or poem to explain what it says directly and to make logical guesses about what is not directly stated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and nonfiction books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts handle similar ideas or topics to build understanding or see how authors approach them differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information presented in different ways, such as in pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is included and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
