description_plain: Your child can analyze how parts of a text, like sentences and paragraphs, fit together and contribute to the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including technical or figurative language, and see how word choices affect meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, or ideas change and interact throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using specific evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use grade-appropriate vocabulary, including subject-specific words, and look up meanings when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., stingy, scrimping, economical, unwasteful, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference in feeling or meaning between words that have similar definitions (like 'stingy' and 'thrifty').

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use how words are related (like cause and effect or part and whole) to better understand what each word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., personification) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech, like when something is described as if it were a person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can double-check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it's used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use reference materials, like dictionaries or online tools, to find out how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., audience, auditory, audible).
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a word means by looking at common Greek or Latin word parts, like prefixes, suffixes, and roots.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies, like context clues or breaking the word into parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child will keep the same style and tone throughout their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child will use different types of sentences to make their writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use language rules correctly when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, parentheses, and dashes to set off extra information in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others' writing and speaking, and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child can notice when their own or others' writing or speaking doesn't follow standard English and knows how to fix it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix pronouns that are unclear about who or what they refer to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and correct mistakes when pronouns don't match in number or person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'myself' or 'ourselves' correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, possessive).
description_plain: Your child can use the right form of pronouns, like 'he,' 'him,' or 'his,' depending on how they are used in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for schoolwork and can figure out the meaning of important words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what unfamiliar words mean by using clues from the sentence, breaking words into parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use their understanding of language to choose words and styles that fit different situations and to better understand what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use proper grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, images, music, sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child can use things like pictures, music, or sounds in presentations to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can give presentations that are organized, use clear details, and speak clearly with good eye contact and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between arguments that are backed up with reasons and evidence and those that are not when listening to a speaker.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information that is shown in different ways, like in pictures, charts, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand different points of view by thinking about and restating key ideas from a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions in detail during discussions to help the conversation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow discussion rules, set goals and deadlines, and take on roles when working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or studied the material, and uses evidence from it to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, build on others' ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can adjust how they speak for different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals to help explain information and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence in a way that is organized and easy for others to follow, using the right style for the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and how well they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information given in different ways, like through pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and take part in conversations with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly for both long-term projects and shorter assignments, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use facts from informational texts to support their analysis, thoughts, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find information from different sources, check if it's trustworthy, and use it correctly in their own work without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer their own or assigned questions, using several sources and coming up with new, related questions to explore further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, share, and clearly show how information and ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from the information or explanation given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal and neutral way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic using well-chosen facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information into categories, and use things like headings, charts, or multimedia to help explain it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces, such as explaining historical events, science experiments, or technical steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can keep a formal style in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect their claims, reasons, and evidence so the writing flows and makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can support their opinions with logical reasons and accurate evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main point, recognize other viewpoints, and organize reasons and evidence in a logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in specific subjects, like science or history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, both for longer projects that need research and revision, and for shorter assignments, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not").
description_plain: Your child can read nonfiction and tell which arguments are supported by good reasons and evidence, and which are not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres [e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories] in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics").
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast different types of stories or books to see how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can find information from different sources, check if those sources are trustworthy, and use the information in their own words while giving credit to the original sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer a question, using several sources and adjusting their focus if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of three pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, share, and work with others, and can type at least three pages at one time.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits the story or events they have described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use specific words, details, and sensory language to help the reader imagine the experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or setting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use storytelling techniques like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop the story and its characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, introducing the narrator or characters, and organizing events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good storytelling techniques and clear event sequences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that follows from the information or explanation given.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use transition words to show how ideas and concepts are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic using facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, organize information clearly, and use things like headings, charts, or multimedia to help explain it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that explain a topic by choosing, organizing, and analyzing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports and follows from their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal, professional way when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their ideas and reasons are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can back up their opinions with clear reasons and trustworthy evidence that shows they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child can state their main point and organize their reasons and evidence in a clear way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their opinions with clear reasons and good evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from books or articles to support their own ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from different sources, check if it is trustworthy, and use it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects to answer specific questions and show what they have learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology, like computers and the Internet, to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways to express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events, using good details and organizing the events in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain complex ideas or information clearly and accurately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that use good reasons and enough evidence to support their opinions about important topics or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of eighth grade, your child can read and understand science and technical texts for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare what they learn from experiments, videos, or other media with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child can combine information from words and visuals (like charts or diagrams) to better understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why the author is explaining, describing, or discussing something in a science or technical text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how each part helps you understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6—8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what scientific or technical words and symbols mean in their reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow step-by-step instructions when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or conclusions in a text and summarize it accurately, without adding their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can use specific examples from science or technical texts to support their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of eighth grade, your child can read and understand history and social studies texts for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different sources (like original documents and later writings) present the same historical topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and judgments in a history or social studies text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from visuals (like charts or maps) with information from written or digital texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can find clues in a text that show what the author thinks or why they wrote it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally).
description_plain: Your child can describe how a history or social studies text is organized, such as by time order or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases, including history or social studies vocabulary, as they are used in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can identify the important steps in a process described in a history or social studies text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or information in a historical source and summarize it accurately, without adding their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can use specific examples from historical sources to support their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6—8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction texts for grades 6–8, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another (e.g., a memoir written by and a biography on the same person).
description_plain: Your child can compare how two authors describe the same events, such as in a memoir and a biography.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge an argument in a text, telling which claims are supported by reasons and evidence and which are not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources, like videos, charts, and written texts, to fully understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a text and explain how the author shows their point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a specific sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the whole text and helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including when they have special, technical, or non-literal meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in a text (e.g., through examples or anecdotes).
description_plain: Your child can explain in detail how a key person, event, or idea is introduced and explained in a text, using examples or stories from the reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and give a summary without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from the text to support their understanding and any conclusions they make.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6—8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems written for grades 6–8, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast how different types of texts, like stories and poems, handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.
description_plain: Your child can compare what it's like to read a story, play, or poem versus listening to or watching it, noticing differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows the narrator's or speaker's point of view in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a specific sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the whole story or poem and helps develop the theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including non-literal meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the plot of a story or play unfolds in different episodes and how the characters react or change as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme or main idea of a story or poem, explain how details show it, and give a summary without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from the story or poem to support their understanding and any conclusions they make.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts handle similar themes or topics and explain how the authors' approaches are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasoning and evidence are strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information presented in different ways, such as in pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is included and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences, paragraphs, and larger sections of a text fit together and relate to the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean, including technical or non-literal meanings, and explain how word choices affect meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says, make logical guesses, and use evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases they learn from talking, reading, and listening to stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words (like walk, march, strut, prance) by acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at school that are colorful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life situations, like noticing colorful places at school when learning the word 'colorful.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).
description_plain: Your child can show they understand common action and describing words by matching them with their opposites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort everyday objects into groups, like shapes or foods, to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child can explore how words are related and learn about the different meanings words can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: Your child can use common word endings and beginnings (like -ed, -s, re-, un-) to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb to duck).
description_plain: Your child can recognize when a familiar word has a new meaning and use it correctly, like knowing 'duck' is a bird and also means to bend down.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words and phrases using what they know from kindergarten reading and lessons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child can spell simple words by sounding them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can write letters that match most consonant and short vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks that come at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child can use a capital letter at the start of a sentence and for the word 'I.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can say and build complete sentences when talking with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).
description_plain: Your child can use common prepositions like to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, and with.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child can understand and use question words like who, what, where, when, why, and how.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (e.g., dog, dogs; wish, wishes).
description_plain: Your child can say the plural form of regular nouns by adding 's' or 'es,' like 'dogs' or 'wishes.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.
description_plain: Your child uses common nouns (like 'cat') and verbs (like 'run') when speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print many uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses a variety of words and phrases, and can figure out new words when reading, writing, or talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands when words have special meanings, how words are related, and the slight differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what unfamiliar words mean by using clues in the sentence, breaking words into parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words that fit the meaning or style needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses proper grammar and language rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly and express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly and share their thoughts, feelings, and ideas so others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions as desired to provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to what they say to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe familiar people, places, things, and events and, with prompting and support, provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child can describe people, places, things, and events they know, and add more details when asked.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions in order to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to get help, find out information, or make sure they understand something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Confirm understanding of a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media by asking and answering questions about key details and requesting clarification if something is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand a story or information by asking and answering questions about important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Continue a conversation through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can keep a conversation going by taking turns talking with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others and taking turns speaking about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules for talking with others, like listening and waiting for their turn to speak.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about kindergarten topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can join group conversations about kindergarten topics with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they talk depending on who they are talking to and the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, charts, or digital media to help explain their ideas and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and ideas in an organized way so others can follow and understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about what the speaker believes and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use information that is shown in different ways, like in pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in different kinds of conversations, sharing their ideas clearly and listening to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember things they have learned or find information to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of books by a favorite author and express opinions about them).
description_plain: Your child can work with others on simple research and writing projects, like reading books by the same author and sharing opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can use computers or tablets to create and share their writing, sometimes working with classmates.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With help, your child can answer questions and use suggestions from others to add more details to their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a single event or several loosely linked events, tell about the events in the order in which they occurred, and provide a reaction to what happened.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, telling, and writing to share a story about something that happened, putting events in order and saying how they felt.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, telling, and writing to explain or give information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces in which they tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book (e.g., My favorite book is…).
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, telling, and writing to share their opinion about a topic or book and explain what they like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write often, sometimes working on longer projects and sometimes on shorter assignments, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or facts to help explain their ideas or answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books or online, check if it is true, and use it in their work without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects to answer questions and show what they have learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology, like computers or the Internet, to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, making changes, fixing mistakes, or trying new ways to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using details and putting events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to teach or explain something, sharing information in a clear and organized way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to share their opinion and give reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent-reader texts with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read simple beginner books and understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that look alike by noticing which letters and sounds are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read common high-frequency words by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).
description_plain: Your child can quickly recognize common words like 'the,' 'of,' and 'you' without sounding them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds with common spellings (graphemes) for the five major vowels.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds and match them to the right letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary or many of the most frequent sound for each consonant.
description_plain: Your child can say the main sound for each letter of the alphabet, especially the consonants.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about letters and sounds to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can change or add a sound in a simple word to make a new word, like changing 'cat' to 'bat.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonent-vowel-consonent, or CVC) words. (This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle, and last sounds in simple three-letter words like 'cat' or 'dog.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together the beginning and ending parts of simple words they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count, say, blend, and break apart the syllables in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say words that rhyme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands how words are made up of smaller sounds and syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can name all the uppercase and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child understands that there are spaces between words when we write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child knows that each spoken word matches a group of letters when written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how books and print work, like how to follow words and recognize letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child will join in group reading activities and understand what is being read.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: With help, your child can find things that are the same and different in two books about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can point out reasons the author gives to explain their ideas in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the text in which they appear (e.g., what person, place, thing, or idea in the text an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures in a book relate to the words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Name the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each in presenting the ideas or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can name who wrote and who drew the pictures in a book and tell what each person did.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can find and name the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don't know in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how two people, events, or ideas in a book are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell what a book is mostly about and share important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important parts of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child will join in group reading activities and understand what is being read.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about how characters in different stories have similar or different adventures.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear (e.g., what moment in a story an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures in a story show what is happening.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, name the author and illustrator of a story and define the role of each in telling the story.
description_plain: With help, your child can name who wrote and who drew the pictures in a story and tell what each person did.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of texts (e.g., storybooks, poems).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between types of books, like storybooks and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about words they don't know in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, and major events in a story.
description_plain: With help, your child can name the characters, where the story happens, and the main events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.
description_plain: With help, your child can retell familiar stories and include important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important parts of a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand both stories and informational books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different books talk about the same topic or idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the reasons and evidence given in a text to decide if they make sense and are important.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and combine information from pictures, charts, videos, and written words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how the author’s point of view or reason for writing affects what is said and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can see how sentences, paragraphs, and sections fit together to make up the whole text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text and how the author’s word choices affect the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect with each other throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme of a text and explain it using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and use examples from the text to support their answers or ideas.

subject: Science
description: Use a computer simulation to model the impact of proposed solutions to a complex real-world problem with numerous criteria and constraints on interactions within and between systems relevant to the problem.
description_plain: Your child can use a computer program to test how different solutions might affect a complicated real-world problem.

description_plain: Your child can judge a solution to a real-world problem by considering things like cost, safety, looks, and effects on people and the environment.
