description_plain: Your child can plan and carry out experiments to compare how different substances are built and figure out how strong the electrical forces are between their particles.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to gather evidence to compare the structure of substances at the bulk scale to infer the strength of electrical forces between particles.
description_plain: Your child can plan and carry out experiments to compare how different substances are built and figure out how strong the electrical forces are between their particles.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to gather evidence to compare the structure of substances at the bulk scale to infer the strength of electrical forces between particles.
description_plain: Your child can plan and carry out experiments to compare how different substances are built and figure out how strong the electrical forces are between their particles.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to gather evidence to compare the structure of substances at the bulk scale to infer the strength of electrical forces between particles.
description_plain: Your child can plan and carry out experiments to compare how different substances are built and figure out how strong the electrical forces are between their particles.

subject: Science
description: Use the periodic table as a model to predict the relative properties of elements based on the patterns of electrons in the outermost energy level of atoms.
description_plain: Your child can use the periodic table to predict how elements will behave based on the patterns of their outer electrons.

subject: Science
description: Use the periodic table as a model to predict the relative properties of elements based on the patterns of electrons in the outermost energy level of atoms.
description_plain: Your child can use the periodic table to predict how elements will behave based on the patterns of their outer electrons.

subject: Science
description: Use the periodic table as a model to predict the relative properties of elements based on the patterns of electrons in the outermost energy level of atoms.
description_plain: Your child can use the periodic table to predict how elements will behave based on the patterns of their outer electrons.

subject: Science
description: Use the periodic table as a model to predict the relative properties of elements based on the patterns of electrons in the outermost energy level of atoms.
description_plain: Your child can use the periodic table to predict how elements will behave based on the patterns of their outer electrons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words that are right for their grade, and look up the meaning of words that are important for understanding or expressing ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., bullheaded, willful, firm, persistent, resolute).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that mean almost the same thing but have different feelings or associations (like 'firm' vs. 'stubborn').

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use how words are related to each other to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g. verbal irony, puns) in context.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of sayings or jokes (like puns or irony) when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can double-check the meaning of a word by using clues in the sentence or looking it up in a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or online tools to find out how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, secede).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of unknown or confusing words using different strategies, like context clues or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active and passive voice and in the conditional and subjunctive mood to achieve particular effects (e.g., emphasizing the actor or the action; expressing uncertainty or describing a state contrary to fact).
description_plain: Your child can use different verb forms (like active, passive, conditional, and subjunctive) to create special effects in writing, such as showing who is doing the action or expressing uncertainty.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use language rules correctly when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child can use an ellipsis (...) to show that something has been left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation marks like commas, ellipses, and dashes to show a pause or break in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb voice and mood.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when the verb tense or mood changes inappropriately in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional, and subjunctive mood.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in different moods (like statements, commands, questions, possibilities, or wishes) correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in both active and passive voice correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what verbals (like gerunds, participles, and infinitives) do in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for school and specific subjects, and can find out what words mean on their own when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of unknown or confusing words by using clues, breaking words into parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use language in different ways depending on the situation, making good choices for meaning or style, and can better understand what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change the way they speak to fit different situations and can use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, videos, or other media in presentations to make their points clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can give a clear and focused presentation, using good evidence and speaking skills like eye contact, loud enough voice, and clear speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a speaker is arguing, judge if their reasons and evidence make sense, and spot when something doesn't fit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why information is shared in different ways (like videos, charts, or speeches) and think about the reasons behind it, such as selling something or sharing an opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child can listen to new ideas from others and, if needed, explain or adjust their own opinion based on what they learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that connect what different people say and answer others with good evidence or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow group discussion rules, keep track of goals and deadlines, and help decide who does what in a group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or researched, and uses what they learned to talk about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 8 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group discussions, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals, like charts or slides, to help explain their ideas in presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information in a way that is organized and easy for others to understand, using the right style for the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and how well they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information that is shared in different ways, like through videos, charts, or speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, both for longer projects that need planning and revision and for shorter assignments, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from nonfiction texts to help explain, reflect on, or research a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find good information from books and online sources, check if it’s trustworthy, and use it in their own words while giving proper credit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and coming up with related questions to explore further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use technology, like the Internet, to write, publish, and clearly show how ideas and information are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new approaches, with some help from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or section that matches and supports the information they presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child can keep a formal and neutral tone in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different kinds of connecting words and phrases to make their writing flow and show how ideas are related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic using facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples that fit well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information into categories, and use things like headings, charts, or pictures to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or inform, including telling about historical events, science experiments, or technical steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or section that matches and supports their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can keep a formal style in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect their claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence so their writing makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can support their claims with logical reasons and accurate evidence from reliable sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their claim, explain how it’s different from other opinions, and organize their reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in subjects like history or science.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write often, both for longer assignments that need research and revision and for shorter tasks, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced").
description_plain: Your child can use reading skills to understand nonfiction, such as judging if an author’s argument and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 8 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new").
description_plain: Your child can use reading skills to understand literature, such as seeing how modern stories use ideas from old myths or religious texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from stories or informational texts to help explain, reflect, or research a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find good information from books and online, check if it’s reliable, and use it in their own words while giving proper credit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer their own questions, using information from different sources and asking more questions to learn even more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to write, share their work, and work together with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or rewriting with help from others, making sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense and connects to the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses specific words and details to help readers picture the events and experiences in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another, and show the relationships among experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and how they are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection to make stories and characters more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by introducing the setting, narrator, and characters, and organize the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up experiences using good details and a clear sequence of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or section that matches and supports the information they have explained.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words, including subject-related vocabulary, to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child explains topics using well-chosen facts, definitions, examples, and quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information into categories, and use things like headings, charts, or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic clearly, using good organization and relevant information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that matches and supports the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal style when making an argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect their ideas and show how their reasons and evidence fit together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their opinions with logical reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their opinion, explain how it is different from other views, and organize their reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments to support their opinions using clear reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes often, both for longer projects that need research and revision, and for shorter assignments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from books or articles to support their own ideas and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and online sources, check if it is trustworthy, and use it in their writing without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects to answer focused questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good details and a clear order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain complicated ideas or information clearly and accurately, using good organization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments to support their opinions using good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of 8th grade, your child can read and understand science and technical texts meant for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare what they learn from experiments, videos, or other media with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child can combine information they read with information shown in charts, graphs, or diagrams.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author explains something, describes a process, or talks about an experiment in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how each section helps you understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6—8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of symbols, key terms, and subject-specific words in science or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow a set of steps when doing experiments, measuring things, or completing technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main ideas or conclusions in a text and give a clear summary that is based only on the text, not their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can point to specific parts of science or technical texts to back up their ideas or explanations.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of 8th grade, your child can read and understand history or social studies texts for their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare a main source (like a diary) and a secondhand source (like a textbook) about the same topic to see how they relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and well-reasoned judgments in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from pictures, charts, maps, or videos with information from written texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can spot clues in a text that show what the author thinks or why they wrote it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally).
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, such as in order, by comparing things, or by showing cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in history or social studies texts, including special vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can pick out the main steps in a process described in a history or social studies text, like how a law is made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or information in a main or secondhand source and give a summary based only on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can use specific evidence from main and secondhand sources to support their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging nonfiction books and articles for their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts on the same topic to see where they disagree on facts or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can explain and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums (e.g., print or digital text, video, multimedia) to present a particular topic or idea.
description_plain: Your child can weigh the pros and cons of using different types of media, like books, videos, or websites, to present information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote something and how they deal with evidence or opinions that disagree with their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child can closely examine how a paragraph is put together and how each sentence helps explain a main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special, figurative, or technical meanings, and see how word choices affect the meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a text makes connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events (e.g., through comparisons, analogies, or categories).
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text shows connections or differences between people, ideas, or events, such as by comparing or grouping them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in a text, explain how it develops, and give a summary that sticks to the facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can point to the strongest evidence in a text to support their understanding, both for what is directly stated and what is implied.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems for their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a modern work of fiction draws on themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works such as the Bible, including describing how the material is rendered new.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a modern story uses ideas, events, or characters from old myths, traditional tales, or religious stories, and describe what is new or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the extent to which a filmed or live production of a story or drama stays faithful to or departs from the text or script, evaluating the choices made by the director or actors.
description_plain: Your child can compare a movie or play version of a story to the original text and discuss the choices made by the director or actors.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how differences in the points of view of the characters and the audience or reader (e.g., created through the use of dramatic irony) create such effects as suspense or humor.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the different viewpoints of characters and the audience create suspense or humor in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more stories are organized and explain how their structure affects the meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular lines of dialogue or incidents in a story or drama propel the action, reveal aspects of a character, or provoke a decision.
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain lines or events in a story move the plot forward, show what a character is like, or lead to a decision.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to the characters, setting, and plot; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea in a story, explain how it develops with the characters, setting, and plot, and give a summary that sticks to the facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite the textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can point to the strongest evidence in a story to support their understanding, both for what is directly stated and what is implied.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts deal with similar themes or topics and see how the authors' approaches are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasoning and evidence are valid and related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can combine and judge information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the parts of a text, like sentences and paragraphs, fit together and help the whole text make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says, make logical guesses about it, and use specific examples from the text to support their answers when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe (e.g., When other kids are happy that makes me happy).
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases they learn from talking, reading, and listening, including using describing words like adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar verbs (like toss, throw, hurl) and similar adjectives (like thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life examples, like describing foods as spicy or juicy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can notice small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use glossaries and simple dictionaries, both in books and online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about words to figure out the meaning of compound words, like birdhouse or notebook.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child can use a familiar root word to help figure out the meaning of a new word with the same root, like addition and additional.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell).
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a new word means when a prefix is added to a word they know, like happy/unhappy or tell/retell.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the rest of the sentence to help figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of unknown or tricky words using different strategies for second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal English (like in school or writing) and informal English (like talking with friends).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use proper language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other reference books to check and fix their spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they have learned to spell new words, like using 'boy' to help spell 'boil.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes correctly to make contractions (like can't) and to show when something belongs to someone (like Sam's book).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly in the greeting and closing of a letter.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters for holidays, product names, and places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences (e.g., The boy watched the movie; The little boy watched the movie; The action movie was watched by the little boy).
description_plain: Your child can make, add to, and rearrange simple and compound sentences to make their writing clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words (adjectives) and action words (adverbs) correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).
description_plain: Your child can use the past tense of common irregular verbs, like 'sat,' 'hid,' and 'told.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'myself' and 'ourselves' correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).
description_plain: Your child can use the correct plural forms of words that don't follow the usual rules, like 'feet,' 'children,' and 'mice.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (e.g., group).
description_plain: Your child can use words that name groups of things, like 'team' or 'group.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses proper grammar when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words and phrases when reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out the meaning of new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words are related, and the slight differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use their knowledge of language to understand how words and sentences work in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses proper grammar and language when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences to give details or explain something when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can record themselves reading stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or describe something that happened, using clear sentences and important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone else says to better understand or learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe the main ideas or details from something they heard or saw.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more information or for something to be explained if they don't understand during a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can add to conversations by connecting their comments to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group discussions, like taking turns and listening carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can join in group conversations about grade-level topics with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change the way they speak depending on who they are talking to and the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures to help explain their ideas and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and ideas in an organized way so others can understand and follow along.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their point of view and reasons for what they say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use information that is shared in different ways, like through pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in conversations, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember things they have learned or find information to help answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report; record science observations).
description_plain: Your child can work with others on research and writing projects, like reading books on one topic or recording science observations.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can use computers or tablets to write and share their work, sometimes working with classmates.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With help, your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by making changes and corrections.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount a well-elaborated event or short sequence of events, include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write a story about something that happened, adding details, using words like 'first' and 'then,' and ending the story clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or give information about a topic, using facts and definitions, and ending with a closing statement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words (e.g., because, and, also) to connect opinion and reasons, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic or book, give reasons for their opinion, use words to connect ideas, and end with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write often, sometimes working on longer projects and sometimes finishing writing in a short time, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction books to help explain their thinking or answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online sources, check if it is true, and use it in their work without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology, like the Internet, to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, editing, and rewriting as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the assignment and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using details and putting events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, using facts and clear organization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to share their opinion and give reasons and evidence to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in a sentence to figure out or correct words they don't know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books out loud smoothly, correctly, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with understanding and for a reason.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that are not spelled the usual way for second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can spot and read words that don't follow the usual spelling and sound rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words with common beginnings (prefixes) and endings (suffixes).

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words with long vowel sounds that are spelled the usual way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read words with common pairs of vowels, like 'ea' in 'team.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple, one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 2—3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand informational books and articles for second and third graders, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences between two texts on the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s reasons support the main points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures or diagrams help them understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can tell what the main purpose of a text is, like what the author wants to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use features like captions, bold words, or headings to quickly find important information in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in texts about second grade topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps are connected in a nonfiction text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multiparagraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a longer text and tell what each paragraph is mostly about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories and poetry, in the grades 2—3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories and poems for second and third graders, with help if needed for harder texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (e.g., Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast different versions of the same story by different authors or cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in a story to show they understand the characters, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.
description_plain: Your child can notice when characters have different points of view and can use different voices when reading their dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is put together, including how it begins and ends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how certain words and phrases add rhythm or meaning to a story, poem, or song.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters react to big events or challenges in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including fables and folktales from different cultures, and explain the main lesson or moral.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different texts talk about similar ideas or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the reasons and evidence an author uses to support their points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use information from different sources, like pictures, charts, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a story or text fit together and help tell the whole story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child understands what words and phrases mean in a story, including special or unusual meanings, and can explain how word choices affect the story.

subject: Language Arts
