description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how people, events, or ideas change and connect with each other throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main idea or theme of a story and summarize the most important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a story says and use details from the text to support their answers or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use new words, including subject-specific terms, and know how to find out what unfamiliar words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference in feeling or meaning between words that have similar definitions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use the relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to better understand what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, biblical, and mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of sayings or references to literature, the Bible, or myths when they appear in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check if their guess about a word’s meaning is correct by looking at how it’s used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses (in print or online) to find out how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what unfamiliar words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies, like context clues, word parts, or reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.
description_plain: Your child can choose words that clearly and briefly express their ideas, avoiding unnecessary repetition.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use language correctly when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives (e.g., It was a fascinating, enjoyable movie but not He wore an old[,] green shirt).
description_plain: Your child can use a comma to separate two adjectives that both describe the same noun.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child can put phrases and clauses in the right place in a sentence and fix sentences where they are misplaced.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child can choose different types of sentences (simple, compound, complex) to show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what phrases and clauses do in a sentence and how they work in specific examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use a wide variety of words, including those needed for school subjects, and can find out what new words mean on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unfamiliar or multi-meaning words by using context clues, word parts, and reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use language in different ways depending on the situation, making good choices for meaning and style, and understanding more when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can adjust how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, videos, or other visuals in presentations to help explain their ideas and highlight important points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can give presentations that clearly explain their ideas, using good examples and speaking clearly with appropriate eye contact and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a speaker is arguing, judge if their reasons make sense, and decide if their evidence supports their points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain the main ideas and details from information presented in different ways, like videos, charts, or speeches.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child can listen to new ideas from others and change their own opinions if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions that encourage others to explain more and can respond thoughtfully to keep the discussion focused.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow discussion rules, keep track of group goals, and help assign roles when working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared, using what they've read or researched to support their ideas and questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can work well in group discussions, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and knows when to use formal English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals, like slides or charts, to help explain ideas and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence in a way that is organized and easy for listeners to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and judge their point of view, reasoning, and how well they use evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information given in different ways, like through pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child will be able to join in conversations with different people, share their ideas clearly, and build on what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use facts and details from informational texts to support their own analysis, thoughts, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and online sources, check if it is trustworthy, and use it in their own words while giving credit to the original source.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and coming up with related questions to explore further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, share, and clearly show how ideas and information are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the topic, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that matches and supports the information they have explained.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child can write in a formal and neutral way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic by adding relevant facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize ideas into categories, and use things like headings, charts, or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain historical events, science experiments, or technical processes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can keep their writing formal throughout their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect their claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child can support their claims with logical reasons and accurate evidence from reliable sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main claim, show how it is different from other views, and organize reasons and evidence in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in specific subjects, using evidence to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and purposes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g. "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims").
description_plain: Your child can use reading skills to look at nonfiction texts, checking if arguments make sense and if the evidence supports the claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history").
description_plain: Your child can compare how a story and a real historical account describe the same time, place, or person to see how fiction writers use or change history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their analysis, thoughts, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and online sources, check if it is trustworthy, and use it in their own words while giving credit to the original source.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and coming up with related questions to explore further.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others, including linking to and citing sources.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and share their work, including giving credit to sources and working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the topic, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits and reflects on the story or events they have described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words, details, and sensory language to describe actions and experiences in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can use different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or setting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use storytelling techniques like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop characters and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, introducing the narrator or characters, and organizing events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using good details and organizing the events clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that fits with and supports the information they have shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will use a formal, respectful tone when writing about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words, including vocabulary related to the topic, to explain things well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses connecting words and phrases to help ideas flow smoothly and show how they are related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by including important facts, definitions, examples, and quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, organize information in a logical way, and use things like headings, charts, or pictures to help explain it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic, organizing and sharing information and ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that fits with and supports the argument they have made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will use a formal, respectful tone when making an argument in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect their claims, reasons, and evidence so their argument makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can support their opinions with logical reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can clearly state their opinion, recognize other viewpoints, and organize their reasons and evidence in a logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments to support their opinions using clear reasons and good evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will practice writing often, both for longer projects that take days and for shorter tasks, for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from books or articles to support their own thinking, research, or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from different sources, check if it is trustworthy, and use it in their own work without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do research projects, both short and long, to answer specific questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways to express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using good details and organizing the events clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain complex ideas or information in a clear and organized way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments to support their opinions on important topics, using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of 8th grade, your child can read and understand science and technical texts meant for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare what they learn from experiments, videos, or other media with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and guesses in a science or technical text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child can combine information from words and visuals like charts or diagrams to better understand a science or technical topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why the author is explaining, describing, or discussing something in a science or technical text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a science or technical text is organized and how each part helps them understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6—8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of symbols and special science or technical words as they are used in the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow step-by-step instructions when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or conclusions in a science or technical text and summarize it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can use specific examples from a science or technical text to support their understanding or analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of 8th grade, your child can read and understand history and social studies texts meant for grades 6–8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a primary source (like a diary or letter) and a secondary source (like a textbook) relate to each other on the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and reasoned judgments in a history or social studies text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from visuals (like charts, graphs, or maps) with information from written or digital texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can spot clues in a text that show the author's point of view or purpose, such as word choices or which facts are included.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally).
description_plain: Your child can describe how a history or social studies text is organized, such as by time, comparison, or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a reading, including special vocabulary used in history or social studies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can pick out the important steps in a process described in a history or social studies text, like how a law is made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or important information in a source and give a clear summary without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can point to specific parts of a text to back up their understanding of primary and secondary sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles written for grades 6–8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two or more authors writing about the same topic use different evidence or ideas to present information in their own way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments and claims in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence make sense and are strong enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject (e.g., how the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the words).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text to its audio, video, or multimedia version and explain how each one shows the subject differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how the author shows their position compared to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the way a text is organized helps develop its ideas and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and explain how word choices affect meaning and mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas in a text affect each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how they develop, and give an objective summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use several pieces of evidence from a text to support their understanding and any conclusions they make.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles written for grades 6–8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare a fictional story about a time or place with a real historical account to see how authors use or change history in their stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium (e.g., lighting, sound, color, or camera focus and angles in a film).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem to its audio, movie, or staged version and explain how each one uses different techniques to tell the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters' or narrators' points of view in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a drama's or poem's form or structure (e.g., soliloquy, sonnet) contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the structure or form of a play or poem (like a soliloquy or sonnet) helps create its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of rhymes and other repetitions of sounds (e.g., alliteration) on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including creative uses, and explain how rhymes and repeated sounds affect a poem or story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact (e.g., how setting shapes the characters or plot).
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a story or play, like the setting, characters, or plot, affect each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea in a story and explain how it develops, then give an objective summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use several pieces of evidence from a story to support their understanding and any conclusions they make.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different texts talk about similar ideas or topics and explain how the authors' approaches are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can outline and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasoning and evidence are strong and make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information presented in different ways, like in pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author's point of view or purpose affects the way a text is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the structure of a text, like how sentences and paragraphs fit together, helps create its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative uses, and explain how word choices affect meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and use specific evidence from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition, and other logical relationships (e.g., however, although, nevertheless, similarly, moreover, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can correctly use grade-level words and phrases, including those that show relationships like contrast or addition (for example, 'however,' 'although,' 'similarly').

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, homographs) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use the relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to better understand what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain the meanings of common sayings, idioms, and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language, like similes and metaphors, in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses (in print or online) to find out how to say a word and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., photograph, photosynthesis).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., cause/effect relationships and comparisons in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a word or phrase means by looking at how it is used in a sentence or story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 5 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies, like using clues from the text or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English (e.g., dialects, registers) used in stories, dramas, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain the differences in the ways English is spoken or written in different stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or combine them to make writing clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use language correctly when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child will spell words correctly for their grade and use a dictionary or other resources if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child will use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to show the titles of books, stories, or other works.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no (e.g., Yes, thank you), to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence (e.g., It's true, isn't it?), and to indicate direct address (e.g., Is that you, Steve?).
description_plain: Your child will use commas correctly when writing yes or no, asking questions at the end of a sentence, or talking directly to someone in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child will use a comma after the first part of a sentence when it introduces the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to separate items in a series.
description_plain: Your child will use commas to separate items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child will use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correlative conjunctions (e.g., either/or, neither/nor).
description_plain: Your child can use word pairs like either/or and neither/nor correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when the verb tense changes incorrectly in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states, and conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use different verb tenses to show when things happen or to describe different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the perfect (e.g., I had walked; I have walked; I will have walked) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use perfect verb tenses like 'I had walked,' 'I have walked,' and 'I will have walked' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child can explain what conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child will use correct grammar and language when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of words for schoolwork and can figure out new words on their own when reading or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and can use figurative language, notice how words are related, and understand slight differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, breaking words into parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use their knowledge of language to choose words and styles that fit different situations and understand what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child will use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child will use correct grammar and language when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can change the way they speak to fit different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, sound) and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or other media to presentations to help explain their main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can give a clear report or opinion, organize their ideas in order, use facts and details, and speak at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can summarize what a speaker says and explain how the speaker supports their points with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information they hear or see, whether it's read aloud, shown in pictures, or presented in other ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child can review the main ideas from a discussion and draw conclusions based on what was learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions in discussions and build on what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow the rules for group discussions and do their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared and uses what they know to share ideas about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 5 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of discussions, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak for different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital media and visual aids to help explain information and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence in a way that is organized and easy for listeners to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker’s point of view and how well they support their ideas with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information that is presented in different ways, like through pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join in conversations, build on others’ ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly, sometimes working on longer projects over several days and sometimes writing shorter pieces, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to informational texts (e.g., "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point[s]").
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in informational texts, and can identify which evidence supports which point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or a drama, drawing on specific details in the text [e.g., how characters interact]").
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast characters, settings, or events in stories by using specific details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction texts to support their ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can find and remember important information from their own experiences or from books and online sources, summarize it in their own words, and list where they found it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects using several sources to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of two pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: With some help, your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others, and can type at least two pages at one time.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a good ending that fits the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use specific words and details that help readers picture the experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events in their story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description, and pacing, to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, descriptions, and pacing to show what happens and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by introducing the situation and characters, and organize the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up experiences using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and subject-specific vocabulary to explain or teach about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., in contrast, especially).
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas within and between sections of information using linking words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by using facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, group related information, and use things like headings, pictures, or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic clearly, sharing ideas and information in an organized way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that matches the opinion they presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., consequently, specifically).
description_plain: Your child can connect their opinion and reasons using linking words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons for their opinion and support them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic or text, state their opinion, and organize their ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic or text and support it with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, sometimes over several days for bigger projects and sometimes in one sitting for shorter tasks, for different purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction texts to support their ideas, reflections, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find important information from different books and online sources, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up experiences using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain complex ideas and information clearly and accurately, using good organization and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments to support their ideas about important topics or texts, using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the text to figure out or correct words they don't know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts with understanding and a clear purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use their knowledge of letter sounds, syllables, and word parts to read long or unfamiliar words, both in and out of context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use fifth-grade phonics and word skills to figure out how to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 4—5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles about history, science, and other topics at a fifth-grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from several books or articles on the same topic to write or talk about it in detail.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support their points in a text, and can tell which evidence supports which idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different books, websites, or other sources to quickly find answers or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child can compare different accounts of the same event or topic and notice important similarities and differences in how people see things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two or more texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts are organized, such as by time order, comparing things, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what general and subject-specific words mean in fifth-grade texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, ideas, or concepts are connected in a history, science, or technical text using details from what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how details support them, and summarize what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote exactly from a text to explain what it says and to support their own ideas about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 4—5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at a fifth-grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre (e.g., mysteries and adventure stories) on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast stories of the same type, like mysteries or adventure stories, to see how they handle similar ideas and topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or beauty of a text (e.g., graphic novel, multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, poem).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures, videos, or other media add to the meaning, mood, or beauty of a story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view affects how the story is told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters, scenes, or stanzas fit together to make up the whole story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes.
