description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including when the author uses comparisons like metaphors or similes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., how characters interact).
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or play, using details from what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem, explain how characters respond to challenges, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote exactly from a story or poem to explain what it says and to support their own ideas about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics to learn more or see how the authors approach them differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are strong enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information presented in different ways, such as through pictures, charts, numbers, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is included and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how sentences, paragraphs, and larger sections of a text fit together and support the whole piece.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in context, including technical and figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says, make logical guesses, and use specific evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use a wide range of academic and subject-specific words, and can figure out the meaning of new words on their own when reading, writing, speaking, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use a wide range of academic and subject-specific words, and can figure out the meaning of new words on their own when reading, writing, speaking, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child will understand the subtle differences in meaning between words that seem similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child will understand the subtle differences in meaning between words that seem similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., euphemism, oxymoron) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child will understand and explain figures of speech, like euphemisms or oxymorons, and how they are used in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., euphemism, oxymoron) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child will understand and explain figures of speech, like euphemisms or oxymorons, and how they are used in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child will understand figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child will understand figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child will check if their guess about a word’s meaning is correct by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child will check if their guess about a word’s meaning is correct by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, or its etymology.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other reference tools, both in books and online, to find out how to say a word, what it means, what kind of word it is, or where it comes from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, or its etymology.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other reference tools, both in books and online, to find out how to say a word, what it means, what kind of word it is, or where it comes from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., analyze, analysis, analytical; advocate, advocacy).
description_plain: Your child understands how words change form to show different meanings or parts of speech, like 'analyze,' 'analysis,' and 'analytical,' and uses them correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., analyze, analysis, analytical; advocate, advocacy).
description_plain: Your child understands how words change form to show different meanings or parts of speech, like 'analyze,' 'analysis,' and 'analytical,' and uses them correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a word or phrase means by looking at how it is used in a sentence or paragraph.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a word or phrase means by looking at how it is used in a sentence or paragraph.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 9—10 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or confusing words and phrases in their reading by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 9—10 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or confusing words and phrases in their reading by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and edit work so that it conforms to the guidelines in a style manual (e.g., MLA Handbook, Turabian's Manual for Writers) appropriate for the discipline and writing type.
description_plain: Your child will write and edit their work to follow the rules in a style guide, like MLA or Turabian, that fits the subject and type of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and edit work so that it conforms to the guidelines in a style manual (e.g., MLA Handbook, Turabian's Manual for Writers) appropriate for the discipline and writing type.
description_plain: Your child will write and edit their work to follow the rules in a style guide, like MLA or Turabian, that fits the subject and type of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can make good choices in writing or speaking to be clear and effective.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can make good choices in writing or speaking to be clear and effective.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a colon to introduce a list or quotation.
description_plain: Your child can use a colon to start a list or a quotation in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a colon to introduce a list or quotation.
description_plain: Your child can use a colon to start a list or a quotation in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a semicolon (and perhaps a conjunctive adverb) to link two or more closely related independent clauses.
description_plain: Your child can use a semicolon (and sometimes a connecting word) to join two closely related sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a semicolon (and perhaps a conjunctive adverb) to link two or more closely related independent clauses.
description_plain: Your child can use a semicolon (and sometimes a connecting word) to join two closely related sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use various types of phrases (noun, verb, adjectival, adverbial, participial, prepositional, absolute) and clauses (independent, dependent; noun, relative, adverbial) to convey specific meanings and add variety and interest to writing or presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of phrases and clauses to make their writing or presentations clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use various types of phrases (noun, verb, adjectival, adverbial, participial, prepositional, absolute) and clauses (independent, dependent; noun, relative, adverbial) to convey specific meanings and add variety and interest to writing or presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use different types of phrases and clauses to make their writing or presentations clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use parallel structure.
description_plain: Your child can use parallel structure, which means making similar parts of a sentence match in form.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use parallel structure.
description_plain: Your child can use parallel structure, which means making similar parts of a sentence match in form.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can figure out new words on their own when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author draws on and transforms source material in a specific work (e.g., how Shakespeare treats a theme or topic from Ovid or the Bible or how a later author draws on a play by Shakespeare).
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses ideas or stories from other works and changes them in their own writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the representation of a subject or a key scene in two different artistic mediums, including what is emphasized or absent in each treatment (e.g., Auden's "Musée des Beaux Arts" and Breughel's Landscape with the Fall of Icarus).
description_plain: Your child can compare how the same subject or scene is shown in two different forms, like a poem and a painting, and notice what each one focuses on or leaves out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the representation of a subject or a key scene in two different artistic mediums, including what is emphasized or absent in each treatment (e.g., Auden's "Musée des Beaux Arts" and Breughel's Landscape with the Fall of Icarus).
description_plain: Your child can compare how the same subject or scene is shown in two different forms, like a poem and a painting, and notice what each one focuses on or leaves out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a particular point of view or cultural experience reflected in a work of literature from outside the United States, drawing on a wide reading of world literature.
description_plain: Your child understands and can explain the point of view or cultural background shown in a story from another country.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a particular point of view or cultural experience reflected in a work of literature from outside the United States, drawing on a wide reading of world literature.
description_plain: Your child understands and can explain the point of view or cultural background shown in a story from another country.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure a text, order events within it (e.g., parallel plots), and manipulate time (e.g., pacing, flashbacks) create such effects as mystery, tension, or surprise.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author arranges a story, uses time (like flashbacks), and creates effects like mystery or surprise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure a text, order events within it (e.g., parallel plots), and manipulate time (e.g., pacing, flashbacks) create such effects as mystery, tension, or surprise.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author arranges a story, uses time (like flashbacks), and creates effects like mystery or surprise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language evokes a sense of time and place; how it sets a formal or informal tone).
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including special or poetic meanings, and explain how word choices affect the story’s mood and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language evokes a sense of time and place; how it sets a formal or informal tone).
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including special or poetic meanings, and explain how word choices affect the story’s mood and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how complex characters (e.g., those with multiple or conflicting motivations) develop over the course of a text, interact with other characters, and advance the plot or develop the theme.
description_plain: Your child can explain how complex characters change, interact with others, and help move the story or theme forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how complex characters (e.g., those with multiple or conflicting motivations) develop over the course of a text, interact with other characters, and advance the plot or develop the theme.
description_plain: Your child can explain how complex characters change, interact with others, and help move the story or theme forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze in detail its development over the course of the text, including how it emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea in a story, explain how it develops using details, and give an unbiased summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze in detail its development over the course of the text, including how it emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea in a story, explain how it develops using details, and give an unbiased summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use strong evidence from the story to support their understanding and any conclusions they make.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use strong evidence from the story to support their understanding and any conclusions they make.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics and explain how the authors' approaches are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics and explain how the authors' approaches are alike or different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the strength of arguments and evidence in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the strength of arguments and evidence in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information that is presented in different ways, such as in pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information that is presented in different ways, such as in pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or purpose affects the way a text is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or purpose affects the way a text is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, fit together and support the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, fit together and support the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or poetic meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or poetic meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme in a text, explain how it develops, and summarize the most important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme in a text, explain how it develops, and summarize the most important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make reasonable guesses, using examples from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make reasonable guesses, using examples from the text to support their answers.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out fair tests in which variables are controlled and failure points are considered to identify aspects of a model or prototype that can be improved.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do fair experiments to test a model or prototype, making sure to control variables and look for ways to improve it.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple possible solutions to a problem based on how well each is likely to meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can come up with different solutions to a problem and compare them to see which one works best.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem reflecting a need or a want that includes specified criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple problem that needs to be solved, including what success looks like and any limits on materials, time, or cost.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a design solution that reduces the impacts of a weather-related hazard.
description_plain: Your child can explain why a certain design helps reduce the harm caused by dangerous weather.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe climates in different regions of the world.
description_plain: Your child can gather and combine information to describe what the climate is like in different parts of the world.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in tables and graphical displays to describe typical weather conditions expected during a particular season.
description_plain: Your child can use tables and graphs to show what the weather is usually like during a certain season.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation for how the variations in characteristics among individuals of the same species may provide advantages in surviving, finding mates, and reproducing.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain how differences among animals of the same kind can help them survive, find mates, and have babies.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to support the explanation that traits can be influenced by the environment.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to show that the environment can affect an animal's or plant's traits.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence that plants and animals have traits inherited from parents and that variation of these traits exists in a group of similar organisms.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to show that plants and animals get traits from their parents and that there are differences among individuals in a group.

subject: Science
description: Develop models to describe that organisms have unique and diverse life cycles but all have in common birth, growth, reproduction, and death.
description_plain: Your child can make models to show that all living things have a life cycle that includes being born, growing, reproducing, and dying, even though each life cycle is unique.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a solution to a problem caused when the environment changes and the types of plants and animals that live there may change.
description_plain: Your child can explain whether a solution works to help plants and animals when their environment changes.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that in a particular habitat some organisms can survive well, some survive less well, and some cannot survive at all.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain that in a certain habitat, some living things do well, some do less well, and some cannot survive at all.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from fossils to provide evidence of the organisms and the environments in which they lived long ago.
description_plain: Your child can look at fossil data to learn about what kinds of plants and animals lived long ago and what their environment was like.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that some animals form groups that help members survive.
description_plain: Your child can explain, using evidence, that some animals live in groups to help each other survive.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem that can be solved by applying scientific ideas about magnets.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple problem that can be solved using what they know about magnets.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to determine cause and effect relationships of electric or magnetic interactions between two objects not in contact with each other.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to figure out how electricity or magnets can cause things to move, even when they aren't touching.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements of an object's motion to provide evidence that a pattern can be used to predict future motion.
description_plain: Your child can watch and measure how something moves to find patterns that help predict how it will move next.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to provide evidence of the effects of balanced and unbalanced forces on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do experiments to show how balanced and unbalanced forces change how something moves.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (e.g., because).
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening, including using words like 'because' to show simple relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (e.g., large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar verbs (like look, peek, stare) and adjectives (like large, gigantic) by explaining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at home that are cozy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life situations, like saying which places at home are cozy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., a duck is a bird that swims; a tiger is a large cat with stripes).
description_plain: Your child can define words by their group and main features, like saying a duck is a bird that swims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories (e.g., colors, clothing) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups, like colors or clothing, to better understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child can show they understand how words are related and the small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can spot common root words (like 'look') and their different forms (like 'looks,' 'looked,' 'looking').

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common word parts (like prefixes or suffixes) to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the rest of the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words and phrases using different strategies for first graders.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child can spell words they haven't learned yet by sounding them out and using what they know about spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child can spell common words correctly, including words with regular patterns and words that don't follow the rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas in dates and to separate words in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use the right punctuation at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dates and names of people.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters for dates and people's names.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can make and expand different types of sentences (statements, questions, commands, and exclamations) when given a prompt.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., during, beyond, toward).
description_plain: Your child can use common prepositions like 'during,' 'beyond,' and 'toward' in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use determiners (e.g., articles, demonstratives).
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'the,' 'this,' and 'those' to help make sentences clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'and,' 'but,' 'or,' 'so,' and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words, like 'big' or 'happy,' in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
description_plain: Your child can use action words to show if something happened in the past, is happening now, or will happen in the future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I, me, my; they, them, their; anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'I,' 'me,' 'my,' 'they,' 'them,' 'their,' and words like 'anyone' or 'everything' correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops; We hop).
description_plain: Your child can make sure the subject and verb match in simple sentences, like 'He hops' or 'We hop.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use regular nouns, names, and words that show ownership, like 'dog,' 'Sarah,' or 'Sarah's book.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses basic grammar rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words for school and everyday topics, and can figure out what new words mean when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands when words have special or playful meanings, and can notice how words are related or have different shades of meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what unfamiliar words mean by using clues from the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use language in different ways depending on the situation, and can understand more when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capital letters, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses proper grammar and language rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas, thoughts, or feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child can clearly describe people, places, things, or events with important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to better understand or learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details from something read aloud or shown in other ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions if they are confused about what is being discussed or read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations by listening and responding to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group discussions, like listening carefully and taking turns to talk.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups
description_plain: Your child can take part in group conversations about first grade topics with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on who they are talking to and the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, charts, or digital tools to help share information and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information in a way that others can follow and understand, using the right style for the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge what a speaker is saying and how they support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and combine information from things they see, hear, or read in different formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in conversations with different people, sharing their ideas clearly and listening to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember things they have learned or find information to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of "how-to" books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).
description_plain: Your child can work with others on research and writing projects, like reading 'how-to' books and writing instructions together.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can use computers or tablets to write and share their work, sometimes working with classmates.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With help, your child can stay on topic, answer questions, and add more details to improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount two or more appropriately sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about things that happened, putting events in order and using words like 'first' and 'then.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, giving some facts and wrapping up their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion, say why they feel that way, and finish their writing with a closing sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write often, sometimes working on longer pieces over several days and sometimes finishing shorter pieces in one sitting, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or facts they read to help explain their thoughts or answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books or online, check if it is trustworthy, and use it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do simple research projects by asking questions and finding out information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child will use computers or the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, editing, and rewriting when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child will write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using details and putting events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, using facts and clear organization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to share their opinion and give reasons and examples to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the story to figure out or fix words they don't know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books out loud smoothly, with the right speed and expression, after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with understanding and for a purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that don't follow regular spelling rules for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have different endings, like -s, -ed, or -ing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read two-syllable words by breaking them into parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child understands that every syllable has a vowel sound and can count the syllables in a word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows how silent 'e' and vowel pairs can make long vowel sounds in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read simple one-syllable words that are spelled regularly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how certain letter pairs, like 'ch' or 'sh,' make special sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use first grade phonics skills to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can break apart a simple word into each of its individual sounds when saying it out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can pick out and say the first, middle, and last sounds in a simple word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can put together separate sounds to say a simple word out loud, even with blends like 'st' or 'bl.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands how words are made up of syllables and individual sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child can tell what makes a sentence, like knowing the first word, using capital letters, and ending punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works, like reading left to right and knowing what a sentence looks like.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read informational texts appropriately complex for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read nonfiction books that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: Your child can find what is the same and what is different between two books about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can point out the reasons an author gives to explain their ideas in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details in a book to talk about its main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between information from pictures and information from words in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can use things like headings, tables of contents, or icons to find important information in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to figure out what words and phrases mean in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how two people, events, ideas, or facts are connected in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell what a book is mostly about and share important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read prose and poetry of appropriate complexity for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read stories and poems that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how characters in different stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details in a story to describe the characters, setting, or what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information, drawing on a wide reading of a range of text types.
description_plain: Your child can explain the difference between storybooks and books that give information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or help you imagine with your senses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can talk about the characters, places, and main events in a story using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their central message or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including important details, and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important parts of what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand both stories and informational books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two books talk about the same topic or idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about reasons and evidence an author gives to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use information from pictures, charts, and words together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author’s point of view or reason for writing affects the story or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a book or story fit together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including special or unusual meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, or ideas change and connect in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme in a text and summarize the most important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and use examples from the text to explain their thinking.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.
description_plain: Your child can look at test results from two objects made to solve the same problem and compare how well each one works.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.
description_plain: Your child can draw or build a simple model to show how an object’s shape helps it do its job.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation people want to change to define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, make observations, and gather information to describe a simple problem people want to solve.

subject: Science
description: Make observations at different times of year to relate the amount of daylight to the time of year.
description_plain: Your child can observe and explain how the amount of daylight changes at different times of the year.

subject: Science
description: Use observations of the sun, moon, and stars to describe patterns that can be predicted.
description_plain: Your child can use observations of the sun, moon, and stars to describe patterns that repeat and can be predicted.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that young plants and animals are like, but not exactly like, their parents.
description_plain: Your child can observe and explain that young plants and animals are similar to their parents but not exactly the same.

subject: Science
description: Read texts and use media to determine patterns in behavior of parents and offspring that help offspring survive.
description_plain: Your child can read or watch information to find patterns in how parents and babies behave to help the babies survive.

subject: Science
description: Use materials to design a solution to a human problem by mimicking how plants and/or animals use their external parts to help them survive, grow, and meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can use materials to create a solution to a problem by copying how plants or animals use their body parts to survive.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a device that uses light or sound to solve the problem of communicating over a distance.
description_plain: Your child can design and build something that uses light or sound to help people communicate over a distance.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to determine the effect of placing objects made with different materials in the path of a beam of light.
description_plain: Your child can test what happens when different materials are placed in the path of a light beam.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to construct an evidence-based account that objects can be seen only when illuminated.
description_plain: Your child can observe and explain that we can only see objects when there is light shining on them.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct investigations to provide evidence that vibrating materials can make sound and that sound can make materials vibrate.
description_plain: Your child can do experiments to show that vibrating things make sound and that sound can make things vibrate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships (e.g., After dinner that night we went looking for them).
description_plain: Your child can use and understand words and phrases that are right for third grade, including words about time and place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that are similar but show different feelings or levels of certainty.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words they learn to real-life situations, like describing someone as friendly or helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).
description_plain: Your child can tell when a word or phrase means exactly what it says or when it means something else.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can notice small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use a glossary or dictionary, in print or online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).
description_plain: Your child can use a familiar root word to help figure out the meaning of a new word with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (e.g., agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of a new word when a prefix or suffix is added to a word they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the rest of the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English.
description_plain: Your child can notice and follow the differences between how we speak and how we write in standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child can choose words and phrases to make their writing or speaking more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use proper language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other reference books to check and fix the spelling of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.
description_plain: Your child can use common spelling patterns, like word families and endings, to help spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (e.g., sitting, smiled, cries, happiness).
description_plain: Your child can spell common words and words they have learned, including when adding endings like -ing or -ed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use possessives.
description_plain: Your child can show when something belongs to someone by using possessive words (like 'the dog's bone').

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in addresses.
description_plain: Your child can use commas in addresses, like '123 Main Street, Springfield, Illinois.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child can capitalize the right words in titles, like the first and important words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'and,' 'but,' 'because,' and 'although' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'bigger' and 'biggest' or 'more quickly' and 'most quickly' correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child can make sure the subject and verb in a sentence match, and that pronouns refer to the right nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in the past, present, and future tense correctly, like 'I walked,' 'I walk,' and 'I will walk.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use both regular verbs (like 'walked') and irregular verbs (like 'ran') correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., childhood).
description_plain: Your child can use words for ideas or feelings, like 'friendship' or 'happiness,' in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can make and use both regular and irregular plural nouns, like 'dogs' and 'children.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs do in sentences and can explain their roles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words for school subjects and everyday life, and can figure out the meaning of new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands expressions that don't mean exactly what the words say, and can tell how words are related or have slightly different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking words into parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about language to choose the right words and style for different situations, and to better understand what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use proper grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences to give details or explain something clearly when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child can make interesting audio recordings of stories or poems, reading smoothly and adding pictures or visuals to help explain details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can talk about a topic, tell a story, or share an experience using clear facts and details, and speak so others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what a speaker says, giving extra information when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
