description_plain: Your child can figure out the main ideas and important details from things they hear or see, like stories, videos, or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain their own ideas and what they understand during a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to make sure they understand, stay on topic, and connect their comments to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group discussions, like taking turns and listening carefully to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or learned about the topic, and uses what they know to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group discussions, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on who they are talking to and can use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals, like charts or pictures, to help explain information in presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and ideas in a way that is organized and clear, so listeners can follow along.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and judge their point of view, reasons, and how they support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and combine information from different sources, like videos, charts, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and take part in conversations with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, sometimes taking several days to plan and revise, and sometimes finishing in one sitting, for different types of writing and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from their own experiences or find it in books and online, take short notes, and organize the information into groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child can use a computer or tablet to write, share, and work with others on their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults or classmates, your child can plan, improve, and edit their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With help, your child can write in a way that is organized and fits the assignment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can end their story in a way that wraps it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'first,' 'then,' and 'finally' to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use conversations and describe what characters do, think, and feel to make the story interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can set up a story by introducing the situation and characters, and organize the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can end their informative writing with a closing sentence or section.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'also,' 'and,' or 'but' to connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by adding facts, definitions, and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, group related information together, and use pictures to help explain when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can end their opinion writing with a closing sentence or section.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'because' or 'for example' to connect their opinion and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons that support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists reasons.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, state their opinion, and organize their reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic and give reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, sometimes taking time to plan and revise, and sometimes finishing quickly, for different types of writing and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction books to support their ideas and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and online, check if it is trustworthy, and use it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects to answer questions and show what they have learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology, like the Internet, to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, editing, and trying new ways to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events, using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic clearly and accurately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to share their opinion and support it with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don't know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud smoothly, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with understanding and for a purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can read third grade words that don't follow regular spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllable words.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read longer words with more than one syllable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have common Latin endings, like '-ful' or '-less.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and understand common beginnings and endings of words, like 'un-' or '-ly.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use third grade phonics and word skills to figure out and read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 2—3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction books about history, science, and other topics at a third-grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences in the main points and important details from two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain sentences and paragraphs in a text are connected, such as showing cause and effect or the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur).
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, maps, or photos along with the words in a text to better understand what is happening and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the author of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the author's opinion in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use things like headings, keywords, or links to quickly find information about a topic in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what general and subject-specific words mean in a third-grade text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps in a process are related by using words about time, order, or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, tell the important details, and explain how those details support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a text and use the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 2—3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at a third-grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about the same or similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show what is happening, set the mood, or highlight parts of a character or setting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own point of view and that of the narrator or characters in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about parts of stories, plays, and poems using words like chapter, scene, or stanza, and explain how each part builds on the previous ones.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including telling the difference between literal and nonliteral language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can describe what characters are like, what motivates them, and how their actions affect what happens in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories from different cultures and explain the main message or lesson using details from the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story and use the story itself to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging fiction and nonfiction books on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different texts talk about similar themes or topics to learn more or see how authors approach them differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the arguments and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information that is shown in different ways, like in pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is included and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how the parts of a text, like sentences, paragraphs, or chapters, fit together and support the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including technical or figurative meanings, and see how word choices affect the meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, or ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says, make logical guesses, and use evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out fair tests in which variables are controlled and failure points are considered to identify aspects of a model or prototype that can be improved.
description_plain: Your child can plan and carry out fair tests by changing one thing at a time and looking for ways to improve a model or prototype.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple possible solutions to a problem based on how well each is likely to meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can come up with different solutions to a problem and compare how well each one meets the requirements.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem reflecting a need or a want that includes specified criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple design problem that needs to be solved, including what success looks like and any limits on materials, time, or cost.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions to reduce the impacts of natural Earth processes on Michigan's people and places.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare different ways to reduce the effects of natural events on people and places in Michigan.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions to reduce the impacts of natural Earth processes on humans.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare different ways to reduce the effects of natural events, like floods or earthquakes, on people.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from maps to describe patterns of Earth's features.
description_plain: Your child can look at maps and use the information to describe patterns in Earth's features, like mountains or rivers.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements to provide evidence of the effects of weathering or the rate of erosion by water, ice, wind, or vegetation
description_plain: Your child can observe or measure to show how weathering and erosion happen because of water, ice, wind, or plants.

subject: Science
description: Identify evidence from patterns in rock formations and fossils in rock layers to support possible explanations of Michigan's geological changes over time.
description_plain: Your child can use patterns in rocks and fossils found in Michigan to help explain how Michigan's land has changed over time.

subject: Science
description: Identify evidence from patterns in rock formations and fossils in rock layers to support an explanation for changes in a landscape over time.
description_plain: Your child can use patterns in rocks and fossils to help explain how a landscape has changed over time.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to describe that animals receive different types of information through their senses, process the information in their brain, and respond to the information in different ways.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show that animals get information through their senses, process it in their brains, and respond in different ways.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that plants and animals have internal and external structures that function to support survival, growth, behavior, and reproduction.
description_plain: Your child can explain that plants and animals have inside and outside parts that help them survive, grow, behave, and reproduce.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model to describe that light reflecting from objects and entering the eye allows objects to be seen.
description_plain: Your child can show how we see things because light bounces off objects and enters our eyes.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple solutions that use patterns to transfer information.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare different ways to send information using patterns, like codes or signals.

subject: Science
description: Develop a model of waves to describe patterns in terms of amplitude and wavelength and that waves can cause objects to move.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how waves have patterns, like height and length, and how waves can make things move.

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe that energy and fuels are derived from natural resources and their uses affect the environment.
description_plain: Your child understands that energy and fuels come from natural resources, and using them can change the environment.

subject: Science
description: Apply scientific ideas to design, test, and refine a device that converts energy from one form to another.
description_plain: Your child can design, test, and improve a device that changes energy from one form to another, like turning sunlight into electricity.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions and predict outcomes about the changes in energy that occur when objects collide.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions and make predictions about what happens to energy when things bump into each other.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to provide evidence that energy can be transferred from place to place by sound, light, heat, and electric currents.
description_plain: Your child can observe and show that energy can move from place to place through sound, light, heat, or electricity.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation relating the speed of an object to the energy of that object.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to explain that faster objects have more energy.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data from tests of two objects designed to solve the same problem to compare the strengths and weaknesses of how each performs.
description_plain: Your child can look at test results from two objects made to solve the same problem and compare what each does well or not so well.

subject: Science
description: Develop a simple sketch, drawing, or physical model to illustrate how the shape of an object helps it function as needed to solve a given problem.
description_plain: Your child can draw or build a simple model to show how the shape of something helps it work to solve a problem.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions, make observations, and gather information about a situation people want to change to define a simple problem that can be solved through the development of a new or improved object or tool.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, observe, and gather information to figure out a simple problem that can be solved by making or improving something.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to obtain information about the purpose of weather forecasting to prepare for, and respond to, severe weather.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to learn why weather forecasts help us get ready for bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Use and share observations of local weather conditions to describe patterns over time.
description_plain: Your child can observe and talk about local weather to notice patterns over time.

subject: Science
description: Use tools and materials to design and build a structure that will reduce the warming effect of sunlight on an area.
description_plain: Your child can use tools and materials to build something that helps keep an area cooler from the sun.

subject: Science
description: Make observations to determine the effect of sunlight on Earth's surface.
description_plain: Your child can observe and describe what happens when sunlight shines on the ground.

subject: Science
description: Communicate solutions that will reduce the impact of humans on the land, water, air, and/or other living things in the local environment.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas for ways people can help protect the land, water, air, or living things nearby.

subject: Science
description: Use a model to represent the relationship between the needs of different plants or animals (including humans) and the places they live.
description_plain: Your child can use a model to show how different plants and animals (including people) need certain places to live.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument supported by evidence for how plants and animals (including humans) can change the environment to meet their needs.
description_plain: Your child can explain, with examples, how plants and animals (including people) can change their environment to get what they need.

subject: Science
description: Use observations to describe patterns of what plants and animals (including humans) need to survive.
description_plain: Your child can observe and describe what plants and animals (including people) need to live.

subject: Science
description: Analyze data to determine if a design solution works as intended to change the speed or direction of an object with a push or a pull.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to see if a design works to change how fast or which way something moves with a push or pull.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to compare the effects of different strengths or different directions of pushes and pulls on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to compare how different pushes and pulls affect how something moves.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use important words for school and specific subjects, and can figure out what new words mean on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use important words for school and specific subjects, and can figure out what new words mean on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain the small differences in meaning between words that seem similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain the small differences in meaning between words that seem similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech, like exaggeration or statements that seem impossible, and tell how they are used in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech, like exaggeration or statements that seem impossible, and tell how they are used in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language, how words relate to each other, and the subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check if their guess about a word's meaning is correct by looking at how it's used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check if their guess about a word's meaning is correct by looking at how it's used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other reference tools, in print or online, to find out how to say a word, what it means, what kind of word it is, where it comes from, or how it is usually used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other reference tools, in print or online, to find out how to say a word, what it means, what kind of word it is, where it comes from, or how it is usually used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., conceive, conception, conceivable).
description_plain: Your child can spot and correctly use different forms of a word to show different meanings or parts of speech, like 'conceive,' 'conception,' and 'conceivable.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., conceive, conception, conceivable).
description_plain: Your child can spot and correctly use different forms of a word to show different meanings or parts of speech, like 'conceive,' 'conception,' and 'conceivable.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11—12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what unfamiliar words mean by using different strategies while reading high school-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11—12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what unfamiliar words mean by using different strategies while reading high school-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references (e.g., Tufte's Artful Sentences) for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.
description_plain: Your child can change how sentences are structured for effect and use this skill to better understand and write complex texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references (e.g., Tufte's Artful Sentences) for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.
description_plain: Your child can use different sentence structures for effect and check references for help, and can understand how sentence structure works in complex reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit, helping them understand what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles that fit, helping them understand what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Observe hyphenation conventions.
description_plain: Your child uses hyphens correctly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Observe hyphenation conventions.
description_plain: Your child uses hyphens correctly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Resolve issues of complex or contested usage, consulting references (e.g., Merriam-Webster's Dictionary of English Usage, Garner's Modern American Usage) as needed.
description_plain: Your child can solve tricky grammar or word usage questions by checking reliable references when needed.

description_plain: Your child can solve tricky grammar or word usage questions by checking reliable references when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply the understanding that usage is a matter of convention, can change over time, and is sometimes contested.
description_plain: Your child understands that grammar and word usage follow rules that can change over time and are sometimes debated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply the understanding that usage is a matter of convention, can change over time, and is sometimes contested.

description_plain: Your child can use a wide range of general and subject-specific vocabulary and can figure out the meaning of new words on their own.

description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unfamiliar words or words with more than one meaning by using clues in the text, word parts, or reference materials.

description_plain: Your child can adjust how they speak for different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools like images, audio, or video in presentations to help explain their ideas and make them more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to enhance understanding of findings, reasoning, and evidence and to add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools like images, audio, or video in presentations to help explain their ideas and make them more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence, conveying a clear and distinct perspective, such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning, alternative or opposing perspectives are addressed, and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and a range of formal and informal tasks.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence clearly, address different viewpoints, and organize their presentation in a way that fits the audience and purpose.

description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence clearly, address different viewpoints, and organize their presentation in a way that fits the audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, assessing the stance, premises, links among ideas, word choice, points of emphasis, and tone used.
description_plain: Your child can judge a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence, and notice how word choices and tone affect the message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and media (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) in order to make informed decisions and solve problems, evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source and noting any discrepancies among the data.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources and formats to make good decisions, checking if the sources are trustworthy and noticing any differences between them.

description_plain: Your child can combine information from different sources and formats to make good decisions, checking if the sources are trustworthy and noticing any differences between them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives; synthesize comments, claims, and evidence made on all sides of an issue; resolve contradictions when possible; and determine what additional information or research is required to deepen the investigation or complete the task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read familiar text comprised of known words.
description_plain: Your child can read simple texts made up of words they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Attend to words in print.
description_plain: Your child will pay attention to the words they see in print.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize 10 or more written words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize at least 10 written words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify letter sound correspondence for single consonants.
description_plain: Your child can match each single consonant letter to its sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the lower case letters of the alphabet
description_plain: Your child can identify all the lowercase letters of the alphabet.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging use of letter-sound knowledge to read words.
description_plain: Your child is starting to use their knowledge of letters and sounds to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in shared reading of informational text including history/SS, science, and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child will join in reading nonfiction books about history, science, or other topics with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a common element between two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find something that is the same in two books about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify points the author makes in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can point out things the author says in a nonfiction book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify illustrations or objects/tactual information that go with a text.
description_plain: Your child can find pictures or objects that go with what is written in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the role of the author and the illustrator.
description_plain: Your child can tell who wrote the words and who made the pictures in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify details in informational text or its graphic representations.
description_plain: Your child can find details in a nonfiction book or in its charts and pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words related to a topic of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find words in a book that are about its main topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify individuals, events, or details in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can point out people, events, or details in a nonfiction book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the topic of the text.
description_plain: Your child can say what a nonfiction book is mostly about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer who and what questions to demonstrate understanding of details in a familiar text.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions about who and what happened in a story they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in shared reading of stories and poetry for clearly stated purposes.
description_plain: Your child will join in reading stories and poems together for a clear reason.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify similarities between two episodes in a story.
description_plain: Your child can find things that are alike in two parts of a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify illustrations or objects/tactual information in print or digital text that depict characters.
description_plain: Your child can find pictures or objects in a book or on a screen that show the characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the speakers in a dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is talking in a conversation in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the beginning and ending of a familiar story with a logical order.
description_plain: Your child can tell where a familiar story starts and ends, and put the events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use rhyming or repetition to identify words that meaningfully complete a familiar story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can use rhyming or repeated words to help finish a familiar story, poem, or song.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the actions of the characters in a story.
description_plain: Your child can tell what the characters do in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using details from the text, recount events from familiar stories from diverse cultures.
description_plain: Your child can retell events from familiar stories from different cultures using details from the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer who and where questions to demonstrate understanding of details in a familiar text.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions about who is in the story and where it takes place in a story they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: (Begins in grade 3)

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify information related to personal experiences and answer simple questions about those experiences.
description_plain: Your child can share information about their own experiences and answer simple questions about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects.
description_plain: Your child can work with others to do simple research and writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, use technology (including assistive technologies) to produce and publish writing.
description_plain: With help, your child can use technology to write and share their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, add more information to own drawing, dictation, or writing to strengthen the message.
description_plain: With help, your child can add more details to their drawing, spoken words, or writing to make their message clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Production and Distribution of Writing
description_plain: Your child can create and share their writing in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select a topic and use drawing, dictating, or writing to compose a message with one fact about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can pick a topic and share one fact about it by drawing, telling, or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select a book and write, draw, or dictate to state an opinion about it.
description_plain: Your child can choose a book and share their opinion about it by drawing, telling, or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia and visual information into presentations.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to their presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select quotes providing relevant information about a topic from multiple print or digital sources.
description_plain: Your child can find and use quotes from different books or websites that give important information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer and pose questions based on one source of information.
description_plain: Your child can do a short research project using one source to answer or ask questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce writing to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers or the Internet to write and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write about events or personal experiences: Use words that describe the feelings of characters or provide other sensory information about the setting, experiences, or events.
description_plain: Your child can write about events or experiences using words that describe feelings or what something looks, sounds, or feels like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to share information supported by details: Write one or more facts or details related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can write and include one or more facts or details about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general academic and domain-specific words and phrases across contexts.
description_plain: Your child can use general and subject-specific words when talking or writing in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language to achieve desired outcomes when communicating. A. Use to-be verbs (am are, is was, were, be, become, became) accurately when writing and communicating.
description_plain: Your child can use language to get their message across and use 'to be' verbs (like am, is, are, was, were) correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate verbs to match nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use the right verb forms to match the subject in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple verb tenses (e.g., I walked, I walk, I will walk).
description_plain: Your child can use simple verb tenses like 'I walked,' 'I walk,' and 'I will walk.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt communication to a variety of contexts and tasks.
description_plain: Your child can change how they communicate depending on who they are talking to or what the situation is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present descriptions, facts, or details supporting specific points made on a topic.
description_plain: Your child can give presentations that include facts or details to support their points about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the argument made by the speaker on a topic.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what argument or main point a speaker is making.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the purpose of information presented in graphic, oral, visual, or multimodal formats.
description_plain: Your child can understand why information is being shared in pictures, talks, videos, or other formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions. A. Come to discussions prepared to share information previously studied. B. Follow simple rules and carry out assigned roles during discussions. C. Remain on the topic of the discussion when asking or answering questions or making other contributions to a discussion. D. Acknowledge new information expressed by others in a discussion and relate it to own ideas.
description_plain: Your child can join group discussions by being prepared, following rules, staying on topic, and connecting their ideas to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of letter-sound relationships and/or common spelling patterns.
description_plain: Your child can spell words by sounding them out and using common spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation and capitalization when writing a sentence or question.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters and correct punctuation at the end of sentences and questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly for different reasons, tasks, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information from literary and informational text to support writing. A. Apply Essential Elements of Grade 8 Reading Standards to literature (e.g., “Compare and contrast themes, patterns of events, or characters across two or more stories or dramas.”). B. Apply Essential Elements of Grade 8 Reading Standards to informational texts (e.g., “Use relevant and sufficient evidence for supporting the claims and argument.”).
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or factual texts to support their writing, such as comparing characters or giving evidence for their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, plan before writing and revise own writing.
description_plain: With help, your child can plan what to write and make changes to improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce writing that is appropriate for the task, purpose, or audience.
description_plain: Your child can write in a way that fits the assignment, purpose, or audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: C. Use temporal words (e.g., first, then, next) to signal order. E. Provide a closing.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'first,' 'then,' and 'next' to show order in their writing and can write a closing sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a narrative about a real or imagined experience introducing the experience, at least one character, and two or more events.
description_plain: Your child can write a story about something real or imagined, introducing the experience, at least one character, and two or more events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to share information supported by details: A. Introduce a topic clearly and write to convey ideas and information about it including visual, tactual, or multimedia information as appropriate. C. Write complete thoughts as appropriate. D. Use domain-specific vocabulary related to the topic. F. Provide a closing.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, share ideas and information about it (including using pictures or other media if helpful), use topic words, write complete thoughts, and end with a closing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write claims about topics or texts. A. Introduce the claim and provide reasons or pieces of evidence to support it. B. Write reasons to support a claim about a topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can write what they believe about a topic or text and give reasons or evidence to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use descriptive words to add meaning when writing and communicating.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words to make their writing and speaking clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of common words to understand the meaning of compound and complex words in which they appear (e.g., birdhouse, household).
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about simple words to figure out the meaning of longer words made from them, like 'birdhouse' or 'household.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the use of multiple-meaning words.
description_plain: Your child understands that some words have more than one meaning and can use them correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Seek clarification and meaning support when unfamiliar words are encountered while reading or communicating.
description_plain: Your child can ask for help or look for clues when they don't know what a word means while reading or talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring root words (e.g., like) and the words that result when affixes are added (e.g., liked, disliked, liking).
description_plain: Your child can use common root words and add beginnings or endings to make new words, like 'like,' 'liked,' or 'disliked.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to determine which word is missing from a content-area text.
description_plain: Your child can use the surrounding words to figure out which word is missing in a reading passage.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding while actively reading or listening to literary nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand what they read or hear in nonfiction books or articles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify where two different texts on the same topic differ in their interpretation of the details.
description_plain: Your child can spot differences in how two texts about the same topic explain the details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the argument made by an author in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what argument or main point an author is making in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine whether a topic is best presented as audio, video, multimedia, or text.
description_plain: Your child can decide if a topic is best shared as audio, video, multimedia, or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author’s purpose or point of view and identify examples from text that describe or support it.
description_plain: Your child can tell why an author wrote something or what their point of view is, and find examples in the text that show this.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate the topic sentence and supporting details in a paragraph.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea and the supporting details in a paragraph.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine connotative meanings of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child understands the feelings or ideas that certain words or phrases suggest in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount events in the order they were presented in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell events from a text in the order they happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a summary of a familiar informational text.
description_plain: Your child can give a summary of an informational text they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite text to support inferences from informational text.
description_plain: Your child can point to parts of an informational text to show where they found their answers or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of text while actively engaged in reading or listening to stories, dramas, and poetry.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand what they read or hear in stories, plays, and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast themes, patterns of events, or characters across two or more stories or dramas.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how themes, events, or characters are alike or different in two or more stories or plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text version of a story, drama, or poem with an audio, video, or live version of the same text.
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem with a version they see or hear, like a video or audio recording.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the difference in the points of view of a character and the audience or reader in a text with suspense or humor.
description_plain: Your child can tell how a character's point of view is different from what the reader or audience knows, especially in funny or suspenseful parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how two or more texts are organized the same or differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine connotative meanings of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child understands the feelings or ideas that certain words or phrases suggest in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify which incidents in a story or drama lead to subsequent action.
description_plain: Your child can point out which events in a story or play lead to what happens next.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount an event related to the theme or central idea, including details about character and setting.
description_plain: Your child can retell an event from a story that connects to the main idea, including details about the characters and setting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite text to support inferences from stories and poems.
description_plain: Your child can point to parts of a story or poem to show where they found their answers or ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add or select drawings or other visual or tactual displays that relate to familiar people, places, things, and events.
description_plain: Your child can add or choose drawings or other visuals that go with familiar people, places, things, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, participate in shared research and writing projects.
description_plain: With help, your child can join in group research and writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce individual or group writing.
description_plain: With help, your child can try out different digital tools to create writing alone or with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, use words acquired through conversations, being read to, and during shared reading activities.
description_plain: With help, your child can use new words they learn from talking, listening to stories, or reading together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of letter and word use.
description_plain: Your child is starting to understand how to use letters and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, provide more information to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: With help, your child can give more information to explain their ideas, thoughts, or feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify familiar people, places, things, and events.
description_plain: Your child can name people, places, things, and events they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate confusion or lack of understanding ("I don't know.").
description_plain: Your child can say when they are confused or don't understand something.

subject: Language Arts
description: During shared reading activities, answer questions about details presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: During group reading, your child can answer questions about details they hear or see.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in conversations with adults.
description_plain: Your child can take part in conversations with adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of conventions of Standard English
description_plain: Your child is starting to understand basic rules for using English correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, identify information related to personal experiences and answer simple questions about those experiences.
description_plain: With help, your child can find and answer simple questions about information related to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, add more information to own drawing, dictation, or writing to strengthen it.
description_plain: With help, your child can add more details to their drawings, spoken words, or writing to make them better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select an event and use drawing, dictating, or writing to share information about it.
description_plain: Your child can pick an event and use drawing, talking, or writing to share information about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select a familiar topic and use drawing, dictating, or writing to share information about it.
description_plain: Your child can pick a topic they know and use drawing, talking, or writing to share information about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select a familiar book and use drawing, dictating, or writing to state an opinion about it.
description_plain: Your child can pick a book they know and use drawing, talking, or writing to say what they think about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of word relationships.
description_plain: Your child is starting to understand how words are related to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging knowledge of word meanings.
description_plain: Your child is beginning to learn what different words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begin to attend to words in print.
description_plain: Your child is starting to notice and pay attention to words in print.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging letter and word identification skills.
description_plain: Your child is beginning to recognize letters and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands that spoken words are made up of syllables and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of the organization of print.
description_plain: Your child is starting to understand how printed words and sentences are organized.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in shared reading of informational text.
description_plain: Your child takes part in reading informational books together.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, match similar parts of two familiar texts on the same topic.
description_plain: With help, your child can match similar parts of two books on the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify points the author makes in a familiar informational text.
description_plain: Your child can point out the main ideas the author makes in a familiar informational book.

description: Identify illustrations or objects/tactual information that go familiar text.
description_plain: Your child can find pictures or objects that go with a familiar book.

description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the words and the pictures in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can find the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, ask a reader to clarify the meaning of a word in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask someone to explain what a word in a book means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify individuals, events, or details in a familiar informational text.
description_plain: Your child can point out people, events, or details in a nonfiction book they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, identify details related to the topic of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can find details about the topic in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify details in familiar text.
description_plain: Your child can find details in books they are familiar with.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, actively engage in shared reading for a clearly stated purpose.
description_plain: With help, your child will join in reading together for a clear reason or goal.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support identify adventures or experiences of characters in a story as same or different.
description_plain: With help, your child can say if characters in a story have the same or different adventures or experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify illustrations or objects/tactual information that go with a familiar story.
description_plain: Your child can find pictures or objects that go with a story they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, identify a speaker within a familiar story.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell who is talking in a story they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, identify a text as telling a story.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell if a book is telling a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, identify sensory or feeling words in a familiar story.
description_plain: With help, your child can find words in a story that describe feelings or senses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify characters and settings in a familiar story.
description_plain: Your child can name the characters and places in a story they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, recount major events in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can retell the main events in stories they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify details in familiar stories.
description_plain: Your child can find details in stories they are familiar with.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select visual, audio, or tactual representations to depict a personal experience.
description_plain: Your child can choose a picture, sound, or object to show something they have experienced.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words acquired through conversations, being read to, and during shared reading activities.
description_plain: Your child can use new words they have learned from talking, listening to stories, or reading together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of conventions of Standard English.
description_plain: Your child is starting to show they understand basic rules for writing and speaking English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of letter and word use.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use letters and words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Combine words when communicating to provide clarification.
description_plain: Your child can put words together to make their meaning clearer when talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a photograph or object that reflects a personal experience and tell one detail about it.
description_plain: Your child can pick a photo or object that shows something they have done and tell one thing about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer questions about the details provided by the speaker.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions about details someone else has shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: During shared reading activities, ask and answer questions about details presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: During group reading or listening, your child can ask and answer questions about what they hear or see.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in conversations with adults and peers.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations with adults and other kids.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate emerging understanding of conventions of Standard English.
description_plain: Your child is starting to show they understand basic rules for writing and speaking English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of letter and word use
description_plain: Your child understands how to use letters and words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select a topic and use drawing, dictating, or writing to compose a message with one fact about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can pick a topic and use drawing, telling, or writing to share one fact about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and use.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and how to use them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of word meanings.
description_plain: Your child knows what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a common element between two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find something that is the same in two books about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create a multimedia presentation of a story or poem.
description_plain: Your child can make a presentation using pictures, sounds, or other media to share a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can contribute to class brainstorming sessions to generate ideas for group writing projects.
description_plain: Your child can help come up with ideas for group writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can choose pieces of information that are related to a given topic in preparation for a class writing project.
description_plain: Your child can pick out information that matches a topic for a class writing project.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can sort information into categories in preparation for a writing project.
description_plain: Your child can sort information into groups to help with a writing project.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort information on a topic or personal experience into two provided categories and write about each one.
description_plain: Your child can sort information or experiences into two groups and write about each one.

subject: Language Arts
