description: The student can identify/use simple visuals (such as charts or illustrations) that go with a particular informational passage.
description_plain: Your child can find or use simple pictures or charts that go with a factual passage.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use visual representations (pictures, charts, diagrams, posters, recipes, calendars, etc.) to find information.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, charts, diagrams, or other visuals to find information.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use information presented visually and/or orally (charts, graphs, diagrams, timelines, photographs, illustrations, posters, recipes, etc.) to answer questions.
description_plain: Your child can use information from pictures, charts, graphs, or spoken explanations to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Answer questions about information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions about information shown in pictures, spoken words, or numbers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare own experience with a written account of the experience.
description_plain: Your child can compare their own experience with how it is described in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the title of a passage.
description_plain: Your child can find the title of a passage.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the title, headings, or illustrations contained within an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find the title, headings, or pictures in a factual text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify elements of informational text (e.g., titles, headings, subheadings, bulleted lists, numbered stems, bold or italicized text, pictures, tables, illustrations, chronology of events, based on fact, cause/effect, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can identify parts of a factual text, like titles, headings, lists, bold words, pictures, or timelines.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify elements that are characteristic of informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can point out features that are common in factual texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine meaning of words in text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words mean when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify an explicit detail that is related to an individual, event, or idea in a historical, scientific, or technical text.
description_plain: Your child can find a clear detail about a person, event, or idea in a history, science, or technical text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the topic of a one- to two-sentence informational text.
description_plain: Your child can say what a short informational text is about.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the main idea of a one-paragraph informational text when it is explicitly stated.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main idea of a one-paragraph informational text if it is clearly stated.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the main idea of a multi-paragraph informational text when it is explicitly stated.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in a longer informational text when it is clearly stated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main idea of a text when it is explicitly stated.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main idea of a text when it is clearly stated.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can answer simple who, what, where, or when questions about short informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can answer simple who, what, where, or when questions about short informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify basic details (e.g., individuals, locations, events, and text features) in a one-paragraph informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find basic details like people, places, events, or text features in a one-paragraph informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can answer questions about details (e.g., individuals, events, locations, and text features) in informational text.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions about details such as people, events, places, or text features in informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify explicit details in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find clear details in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of text while actively engaging in shared reading of stories, dramas, and poetry.
description_plain: Your child shows understanding when reading stories, plays, or poems together.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify what is the same (e.g., between two characters, or between two settings).
description_plain: Your child can tell what is the same between two characters or two settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify what is the same between two characters, two settings, or two events within a paragraph of a narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can find what is the same between two characters, settings, or events in a paragraph of a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify what is similar between two characters, two settings, or two events in a narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can tell what is similar between two characters, settings, or events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare characters, settings or events in stories, myths or texts from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare characters, settings, or events in stories or texts from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text representation of a story and a visual, tactual, or oral version of a story.
description_plain: Your child can connect a written story to a picture, object, or spoken version of the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the speaker in a one- to two-sentence narrative text with dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is speaking in a short story with dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify who is telling a story when given choices.
description_plain: Your child can choose who is telling a story when given options.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the narrator of a story.
description_plain: Your child can say who the narrator is in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the narrator of a story.
description_plain: Your child can identify who is telling the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can differentiate between two types of narrative text genre (e.g., stories, poetry, songs).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between two types of stories, like poems and songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify simple story elements in narrative text (e.g., characters, setting, story sequence).
description_plain: Your child can find simple story parts like characters, setting, or what happens first, next, and last.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the elements that comprise stories (e.g., characters, settings, plots, themes, point of view, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can identify the parts that make up a story, such as characters, setting, plot, theme, and point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify elements that are characteristic of stories.
description_plain: Your child can point out the features that are common in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words mean when reading a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use details from the text to describe characters in the story.
description_plain: Your child can use details from the story to describe the characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the main idea of a one- to two-sentence narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main idea of a very short story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the main idea of a one-paragraph narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main idea of a one-paragraph story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the theme or main idea of a multi-paragraph narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the theme or main idea of a longer story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the theme or central idea of a familiar story, drama or poem.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main idea or message of a familiar story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can answer simple who, what, where or when questions about narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can answer simple who, what, where, or when questions about a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can answer questions about basic elements in a narrative text (e.g., characters’ names, settings, and specific events).
description_plain: Your child can answer questions about basic parts of a story, like characters’ names, where it happens, and important events.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use details to answer questions about narrative text (characters, setting, plot, sequence, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can use details to answer questions about the story, such as who is in it, where it happens, what happens, and in what order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use details from the text to recount what the text says.
description_plain: Your child can use details from the story to tell what it says.

subject: Language Arts
description: Select or create audio recordings and visual/tactile displays to emphasize specific points in a presentation.
description_plain: Your child can pick or make audio recordings and pictures to help make a point during a presentation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify quotes providing relevant information about a topic from multiple print or digital sources.
description_plain: Your child can find quotes with useful information about a topic from different books or online sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct research to answer a question based on multiple sources of information.
description_plain: Your child can do research using more than one source to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce writing to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology, like the Internet, to write and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a word or phrase paired with a picture that describes the feelings of a person/character in a text.
description_plain: Your child can match a word or phrase with a picture that shows how a person or character feels in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify words or a phrase that describes the feelings of the people/characters in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find words or phrases that describe how people or characters feel in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify/use words to describe the feelings of people/characters in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use words that show how people or characters feel in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write about events or personal experiences: Use words that describe feelings of people or characters in the narrative.
description_plain: Your child can write about events or experiences and use words to describe how people or characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can select a fact, detail, or other piece of information that relates to a given topic.
description_plain: Your child can pick out a fact or detail that matches a given topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can select facts, details, or other pieces of information that relate to a given topic.
description_plain: Your child can choose facts or details that are connected to a certain topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify/list/organize facts, details, or other pieces of information to support a topic.
description_plain: Your child can find, list, or organize facts and details that support a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to share information supported by details: Provide facts, details, or other information related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can write to share information and include facts or details about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general academic and domain-specific words and phrases across contexts.
description_plain: Your child can use general and subject-specific words in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the correct use of language to communicate effectively with familiar people (e.g., sharing information, responding to greetings, being polite, and making simple requests, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can use the right words and language to communicate clearly with people they know, like sharing information or being polite.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use language to communicate effectively with a variety of audiences and for different purposes (e.g., asking questions, sharing information, responding to greetings, using polite expressions, using appropriate body language).
description_plain: Your child can use language to talk with different people and for different reasons, like asking questions or using polite words.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use language to communicate effectively with peers and adults (e.g., asking and answering questions, providing more information when someone does not understand the first time, sharing information, making requests, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can use language to communicate well with both peers and adults, such as asking questions, giving more information, or making requests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language to achieve desired outcomes when communicating.
description_plain: Your child can use language to get their message across when talking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate Standard English grammar and usage when communicating: Produce complete simple sentences when writing or communicating.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and write or say complete simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Communicate precisely (i.e., provide complete information) or efficiently (i.e., telegraphic communication) as required by the context, task, and communication partner.
description_plain: Your child can give clear and complete information, or keep it short and to the point, depending on the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present findings on a topic including relevant descriptions, facts, or details.
description_plain: Your child can share what they have learned about a topic and include important facts or details.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can select one or more details from information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can pick out details from information they hear or see in different media.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can distinguish whether a piece of information shared by a speaker is a fact.
description_plain: Your child can tell if something a speaker says is a fact.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can determine whether the information provided by a speaker is based on fact or opinion.
description_plain: Your child can decide if information from a speaker is a fact or just an opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine whether the claims made by a speaker are fact or opinion.
description_plain: Your child can figure out if what a speaker says is a fact or an opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify details related to the main idea of a text presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can find details that support the main idea when listening to or watching something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions. A. Come to discussions prepared to share information. B. With guidance and support from adults and peers, follow simple, agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles. C. Remain on the topic of the discussion when answering questions or making other contributions to a discussion. D. Acknowledge new information expressed by others in a discussion.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group discussions by being prepared, following rules, staying on topic, and listening to others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of conventions of Standard English: Spell words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of letter-sound relationships and/or common spelling patterns.
description_plain: Your child can spell words by sounding them out and using common spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can differentiate if a sentence is a statement or a question.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a sentence is a statement or a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify capital letters and/or basic punctuation (periods and question marks).
description_plain: Your child can spot capital letters and basic punctuation like periods and question marks.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify which word/words should be capitalized in a sentence (first words and proper nouns) and/or choose the correct ending punctuation (period, question mark, or exclamation point).
description_plain: Your child can identify which words need capital letters and choose the right punctuation at the end of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of conventions of Standard English: Use end punctuation when writing a sentence or question.
description_plain: Your child can use the correct punctuation at the end of a sentence or question.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly for different reasons and for different people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information from literary and informational text to support writing. A. Apply Essential Elements of Grade 7 Reading Standards to literature (e.g., “Recognize the difference between fictional characters and nonfictional characters.”). B. Apply Essential Elements of Grade 7 Reading Standards to informational texts (e.g., “Use relevant and sufficient evidence for supporting the claims and argument.”).
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or facts from nonfiction to support their writing, like telling the difference between real and made-up characters or using evidence to back up their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, plan before writing and revise own writing.
description_plain: With help, your child can plan what to write and make changes to improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce writing that is appropriate for the task, purpose, or audience.
description_plain: Your child can write in a way that fits the task, purpose, or audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write about events or personal experiences: Use temporal words (e.g., first, then, next) to signal order.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'first,' 'then,' and 'next' to show the order of events when writing about experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can provide a detail (paired with a picture) about a person/character, while preparing to write/communicate about an event or personal experience.
description_plain: Your child can give a detail about a person or character, often with a picture, when getting ready to write or talk about an event or experience.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can provide details about one person/character or one action/happening that occurred during an event or personal experience, while preparing to write/communicate about it.
description_plain: Your child can give details about a person, character, or action that happened during an event or experience when preparing to write or talk about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can write/draw/dictate about an event or personal experience, including at least one person/character and one action/happening.
description_plain: Your child can write, draw, or tell about an event or experience, including at least one person or character and one thing that happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write about events or personal experiences: Write a narrative about a real or imagined experience, at least one character, and two or more events.
description_plain: Your child can write a story about something real or imagined, with at least one character and two or more events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to share information supported by details: A. Introduce a topic and write to convey ideas and information about it including visual, tactual or multimedia information as appropriate. D. Select domain-specific vocabulary to use in writing about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, share ideas and information about it (using pictures or other media if needed), and use topic-specific words in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write claims about topics or texts. A. Introduce a topic or text and write one claim about it. B. Write one or more reasons to support a claim about a topic or text. C. Use temporal words (e.g., first, next, also) to create cohesion.
description_plain: Your child can write about a topic or text by making a claim, giving reasons to support it, and using words like 'first' or 'next' to connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a word with the opposite meaning as a target word that appears in a short text.
description_plain: Your child can find a word that means the opposite of another word in a short text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a word with the opposite meaning as a target word that appears in text.
description_plain: Your child can find a word that means the opposite of another word in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can demonstrate understanding of words with the same or different meanings (synonyms and antonyms).
description_plain: Your child can show they understand words that mean the same or the opposite (synonyms and antonyms).

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and use: Demonstrate understanding of synonyms and antonyms.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand and use words that mean the same or the opposite.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the meaning of a word or phrase used in a given context given choices of words paired with pictures/objects.
description_plain: Your child can pick the correct meaning of a word or phrase when given choices with pictures or objects to help.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the correct word (from commonly confused words) to use in a given context.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right word to use when given a list of words that are often mixed up.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the meaning of literal or non-literal/figurative words or phrases when presented in the context of narrative or informational text.
description_plain: Your child can tell what a word or phrase means, whether it is being used in a regular or a creative way, when reading stories or information texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and use: Identify the literal and nonliteral meanings of words in context.
description_plain: Your child understands both the exact and creative meanings of words when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of word meanings: Seek clarification and meaning support when unfamiliar words are encountered while reading or communicating.
description_plain: Your child will ask for help or look for clues when they find a word they don't know while reading or talking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of word meanings: Use frequently occurring root words (e.g., like) and the words that result when affixes are added (e.g., liked, disliked, liking).
description_plain: Your child can use common root words and add beginnings or endings to make new words, like 'like,' 'liked,' or 'disliked.'

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use cues to recognize the meaning of familiar words when paired with pictures.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of familiar words when they see them with matching pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use context clues to determine the meaning of familiar words paired with pictures and/or objects.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the story and pictures or objects to figure out what familiar words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use context as a clue to guide selection of a word or words that best completes a sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in a sentence to pick the word or words that fit best.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of word meanings: Use context to determine which word is missing from a text.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the story to figure out which word is missing in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding while actively reading or listening to literary nonfiction.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand what they read or hear in nonfiction books and articles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how different texts on the same topic present the details.
description_plain: Your child can tell how two texts about the same topic are alike and different in the details they share.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how a claim or reason fits into the overall structure of an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a reason or claim fits into the way an informational text is organized.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare a text to an audio, video or multimedia version of the same text.
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the same text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the purpose of an informational/functional text (e.g., to tell about events, give directions, provide information) using words/phrases paired with pictures.
description_plain: Your child can tell why an informational text was written, like to give directions or share information, using words and pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can select words/phrases that show how the author feels about the topic of an informational text or identify why the author most likely wrote it.
description_plain: Your child can pick out words that show how the author feels about the topic or guess why the author wrote the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the author’s point of view or his/her purpose for writing an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can tell what the author thinks or why they wrote an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author’s purpose or point of view.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author’s purpose or point of view in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can answer simple questions regarding a variety of basic informational/functional text (e.g., awareness of daily schedule, calendars, dictionary, phone directories, etc.).
description_plain: Your child can answer simple questions about basic informational texts like schedules, calendars, or directories.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a variety of informational/functional texts and/or text patterns (e.g., problem/solution, sequence/order, same/different, cause/effect, descriptive).
description_plain: Your child can recognize different types of informational texts and how they are organized, like by order, cause and effect, or comparing things.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify how an informational text has been organized by the author (e.g., problem/solution, sequence/order, compare/contrast, cause/effect, descriptive) and/or indicate how a fact, step, or event fits into the text structure.
description_plain: Your child can tell how an informational text is organized and explain how a fact or event fits into that structure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how a fact, step, or event fits into the overall structure of the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a fact, step, or event fits into the way the text is put together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words or phrases are used to persuade or inform a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell how certain words or phrases are used to convince or inform the reader in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify what is the same between two individuals within a short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find what is the same between two people in a short informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify which informational text element is associated with a given text element. (Example: Under which heading does given information go in a chart?).
description_plain: Your child can match information to the correct heading or part in a chart or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can indicate how two individuals, events, or ideas in an informational text are related.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, or ideas in an informational text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how two individuals, events or ideas in a text are related.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how two people, events, or ideas in a text are related.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the main idea of a short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main idea of a short informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify one detail that relates to the main idea of a short informational text (when the main idea is provided).
description_plain: Your child can find one detail that supports the main idea in a short informational text when the main idea is given.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify one or more central ideas in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find one or more main ideas in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use concrete details to answer simple who, what, where, or when questions about short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can use details from a short informational text to answer simple who, what, where, or when questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use concrete details to answer simple questions and make predictions about a short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can use details from a short informational text to answer questions and make guesses about what might happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use details and clues from informational text to answer questions and make inferences.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from an informational text to answer questions and make smart guesses (inferences).

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze text to identify where information is explicitly stated and where inferences must be drawn.
description_plain: Your child can tell when information is clearly stated in a text and when they need to figure it out from clues.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of text while actively reading or listening to stories, dramas, or poetry.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand what they read or hear in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare a fictional time, place, or character in one text with the same time, place, or character portrayed in a historical account.
description_plain: Your child can compare a made-up time, place, or character in a story to the real version in a history text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare a text version of a story, drama, or poem with an audio, video, or live version of the same text.
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem to an audio, video, or live version of the same text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare the points of view of two or more characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more characters or narrators see things in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare the structure of two or more texts (e.g., stories, poems, or dramas).
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts, like stories, poems, or plays, are organized.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of simple idioms and figures of speech as they are used in a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what simple sayings or expressions mean when reading a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify what is the same between two characters when given a visual model to match.
description_plain: Your child can find what is the same about two characters when shown a picture or chart to help.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify which story element is associated with a given story element.
description_plain: Your child can match one part of a story, like a character or setting, with another related part.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can indicate how two or more story elements are related.
description_plain: Your child can show how two or more parts of a story, like characters or events, are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how two or more story elements are related.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different parts of a story, such as characters, setting, or events, are related.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the theme or main idea of a short narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can tell you the main idea or message of a short story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify one detail that relates to the theme or main idea of a short narrative text (when the theme or main idea is provided).
description_plain: Your child can find one detail in a short story that matches the main idea or message when told what it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify one or more details in a narrative text that are related to the text’s theme or main idea.
description_plain: Your child can pick out details in a story that support the main idea or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify events in a text that are related to the theme or central idea.
description_plain: Your child can find events in a story that are connected to the main idea or theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use concrete details to answer simple who, what, where, or when questions about short narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can answer simple questions about who, what, where, or when using details from a short story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use concrete details to answer simple questions and make predictions about short narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions and make guesses about what might happen next in a short story using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can answer questions about narrative text using explicit details and clues to make inferences.
description_plain: Your child can answer questions about a story by using clear details and clues to figure things out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze text to identify where information is explicitly stated and where inferences must be drawn.

description: The student can determine which of two similar shapes or objects is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: The student can select appropriate numbers and/or quantities to solve problems. Quantities limited to no more than 5.

subject: Math
description: The student can select appropriate numbers and/or quantities to solve problems. Quantities limited to no more than 5.
description_plain: Your child can pick the right numbers or amounts (up to 5) to solve simple math problems.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can choose the correct numbers or amounts (up to 50) to solve math problems.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can choose the correct numbers or amounts (up to 50) to solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: The student can extend or describe a number pattern involving doubling, including determining the value of a quantity that is squared to solve a problem.
description_plain: Your child can continue or explain a number pattern that doubles each time, and can find the answer when a number is multiplied by itself.

subject: Math
description: The student can extend or describe a number pattern involving doubling, including determining the value of a quantity that is squared to solve a problem.
description_plain: Your child can continue or explain a number pattern that doubles each time, and can find the answer when a number is multiplied by itself.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital media strategically (e.g., textual, graphical, audio, visual, and interactive elements) in presentations to support understanding and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can use things like pictures, charts, sounds, or videos in presentations to help others understand and stay interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write answers to research questions by selecting relevant information from multiple resources.
description_plain: Your child can answer research questions by picking useful information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct research projects to answer questions posed by self and others using multiple sources of information.
description_plain: Your child can do research using different sources to answer questions they or others have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce, publish and update an individual or shared writing project.
description_plain: Your child can use technology, like the Internet, to create, share, and update their writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a word that describes an experience or event while preparing to write/communicate about it.
description_plain: Your child can pick a word that describes what happened when getting ready to write or talk about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify words that describe an experience or event while preparing to write/communicate about it.
description_plain: Your child can pick words that describe what happened when getting ready to write or talk about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify/use descriptive words to create a vivid picture while preparing to write/communicate about an experience or event.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words to help others picture what happened when preparing to write or talk about an experience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write about events or personal experiences: Use descriptive words and phrases to convey a vivid picture of experiences, events, setting, or characters.
description_plain: Your child can write about events or personal experiences using describing words and phrases to help readers imagine what happened, where, or who was involved.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can select a fact or detail related to a given topic.
description_plain: Your child can choose a fact or detail that matches a given topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can select facts or details that relate to a given topic.
description_plain: Your child can pick facts or details that go with a given topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify/list/organize facts, details, or quotes to support a given topic.
description_plain: Your child can find, list, or organize facts, details, or quotes to support a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to share information supported by details: Develop the topic with relevant facts, details, or quotes.
description_plain: Your child can write to share information and support it with facts, details, or quotes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use general academic and domain-specific words and phrases across contexts.
description_plain: Your child can use general and subject-specific words in different situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the correct use of language to communicate effectively with familiar people (e.g., sharing information, responding to greetings, being polite, and making simple requests, etc.)
description_plain: Your child can use the right words to communicate clearly with people they know, like sharing information or being polite.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use language to communicate effectively with a variety of audiences and for different purposes (e.g., asking questions, sharing information, responding to greetings, using polite expressions, using appropriate body language).
description_plain: Your child can use language to communicate well with different people and for different reasons, like asking questions or sharing information.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use complex language, including compound sentences, to communicate effectively with peers and adults (e.g., asking and answering questions, sharing information, clarifying statements, making requests, etc.)
description_plain: Your child can use more complex sentences to talk with friends and adults, such as asking questions or making requests.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use language to achieve desired outcomes when communicating. A. Vary sentence structure using a variety of simple and compound sentence structures.
description_plain: Your child can use language to get their point across and can use both simple and compound sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate Standard English grammar and usage when communicating: Use digital, electronic, and other resources and tools to improve uses of language as needed.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language, and use tools like computers to help improve their writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify/use the correct noun or action verb when given a picture prompt.
description_plain: Your child can pick or use the right noun or action word when shown a picture.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify/use singular/plural nouns, verb tense and personal pronouns when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use the correct form of nouns (singular/plural), verb tense, and personal pronouns when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify/use correct conventions of Standard English when communicating through writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use proper English rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate Standard English grammar and usage when communicating: Use conventions of Standard English when needed.
description_plain: Your child can use correct English grammar and rules when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt communication to a variety of contexts and tasks using complete sentences when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they communicate to fit different situations and use complete sentences when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present an argument on a topic using an organization appropriate to the purpose, audience, and task.
description_plain: Your child can present an argument on a topic in a way that fits the audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can select one or more details from information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can pick out details from information they hear or see.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the claim/point a speaker makes.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main point a speaker is making.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a claim/reasoning that enhance(s) a speaker’s argument on a topic.
description_plain: Your child can find reasons or points that make a speaker’s argument stronger.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine whether the claims and reasoning enhance the speaker’s argument on a topic.
description_plain: Your child can decide if a speaker’s claims and reasons make their argument better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the credibility and accuracy of information presented across diverse media or formats.
description_plain: Your child can judge if information from different sources is trustworthy and correct.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions. A. Prepare for discussions by collecting information on the topic. B. Work with peers to set rules and goals for discussions. C. Ask and answer questions to verify or clarify own ideas and understandings during a discussion. D. Respond to agreements and disagreements in a discussion.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group discussions by preparing, helping set rules and goals, asking and answering questions, and responding to others’ ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of conventions of Standard English: Spell most single-syllable words correctly and apply knowledge of word chunks in spelling longer words.
description_plain: Your child can spell most one-syllable words correctly and use word parts to help spell longer words.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can differentiate if a sentence is a statement or a question.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a sentence is a statement or a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a sentence that uses the correct capitalization and/or ending punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can spot sentences that use the right capital letters and punctuation at the end.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a grammatically correct sentence that uses correct capitalization, very basic internal punctuation, and ending punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can spot a sentence that uses correct capital letters and punctuation marks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of conventions of Standard English: Demonstrate conventions of Standard English including capitalization, ending punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child will use capital letters, correct punctuation, and correct spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child will write regularly for different reasons and audiences, sometimes taking time to research, think, and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite evidence from literary or informational texts. A. Apply Grades 11-12 Essential Elements for Reading Standards to literature (e.g., “Compare and contrast elements of American literature to other literary works, self, or one’s world. [Compare themes, topics, locations, context, and point of view].”). B. Apply Grades 11-12 Essential Elements for Reading Standards to informational texts (e.g., “Compare and contrast reasoning and arguments used in one's work with those used in seminal U.S. texts.”).
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from stories or informational texts to support their ideas, such as comparing themes or arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, and rewriting.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, editing, and rewriting as needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce writing that is appropriate to a particular task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write in a way that fits the assignment, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write about events or personal experiences. C. Organize the events in the narrative using temporal words to signal order and add cohesion. E. Provide a closing.
description_plain: Your child can write about events in order using words like 'first' or 'next,' and will include an ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write about events or personal experiences: Write a narrative about a problem, situation, or observation including at least one character, details, and clearly sequenced events.
description_plain: Your child can write a story about a problem, situation, or observation, including at least one character, details, and events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can provide one or more details while preparing to write/communicate about a personal experience, problem, or situation.
description_plain: Your child can give one or more details when getting ready to write or talk about a personal experience, problem, or situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can correctly sequence two or more events while preparing to write/communicate about a personal experience, problem, or situation.
description_plain: Your child can put two or more events in the right order when preparing to write or talk about a personal experience, problem, or situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can write/draw/dictate about a real-life problem, situation, or observation that includes supporting details and sequenced events.
description_plain: Your child can write, draw, or tell about a real-life problem, situation, or observation, including details and events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write to share information supported by details: A. Introduce a topic clearly and write an informative or explanatory text that conveys ideas, concepts, and information including visual, tactual, or multimedia information as appropriate. B. See **Claim 4. C. Use complete, simple sentences, as well as compound and other complex sentences as appropriate. D. Use domain specific vocabulary when writing claims related to a topic of study or text. F. Provide a closing or concluding statement.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or share information, using details, clear sentences, topic-specific words, and a closing statement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims. A. Write an argument to support a claim that results from studying a topic or reading a text. B. Support claims with reasons and evidence drawn from text.
description_plain: Your child can write an argument to support a claim about a topic or text, using reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the correct meaning of words that have multiple meanings.
description_plain: Your child can pick the correct meaning of a word that has more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the correct meaning of words that have multiple meanings.
description_plain: Your child can pick the correct meaning of a word that has more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the meaning of literal or non-literal/figurative words or phrases when presented in the context of narrative or informational text, including (but not limited to) multiple-meaning words and common figures of speech.
description_plain: Your child can figure out if a word or phrase means exactly what it says or if it has a special or figurative meaning in a story or information text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and use: Interpret simple figures of speech encountered while reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain simple figures of speech they hear or read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of word meanings: Consult reference materials (dictionaries, online vocabulary supports) to clarify the meaning of unfamiliar words encountered when reading.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or online tools to find out what unfamiliar words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of word meanings: Identify and use root words and the words that result when affixes are added or removed.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use root words and understand how adding or removing word parts changes the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use cues to recognize words that may or may not be familiar, when paired with pictures.
description_plain: Your child can use clues and pictures to figure out if a word is familiar or not.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use context clues to determine the meaning of words that may or may not be familiar.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in the sentence to figure out what an unfamiliar word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use context as a clue to determine word meaning.
description_plain: Your child can use the surrounding words to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of word meanings: Use context to determine the meaning of unknown words.
description_plain: Your child can use the context of a sentence or paragraph to figure out the meaning of unknown words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding while actively reading or listening to literary non-fiction.
description_plain: Your child can understand and follow along when reading or listening to nonfiction books or articles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast arguments made by two different texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast the arguments made in two different texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can indicate own opinion (agreement or disagreement) about the topic of a short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can say whether they agree or disagree with the topic of a short informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a claim made by the author of a short informational text or identify one detail that supports the claim.
description_plain: Your child can find a claim the author makes in a short informational text or find one detail that supports it.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a claim or reason that enhances the author’s argument in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find a claim or reason that makes the author's argument stronger in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine whether the claims or reasoning enhance the author’s argument in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can decide if the claims or reasons in an informational text make the author's argument better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze information presented in different media on related topics to answer questions or solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can look at information from different sources, like videos or articles, on the same topic to answer questions or solve problems.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a word/phrase that describes the author’s meaning/point of view.
description_plain: Your child can find a word or phrase that shows what the author means or thinks.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify a word/phrase that describes the author’s meaning/point of view.
description_plain: Your child can find a word or phrase that shows what the author means or thinks.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify/describe the author’s intended meaning/point of view and discuss how it relates to one’s own point of view.
description_plain: Your child can describe what the author thinks or means and talk about how it is similar or different from their own opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine author’s point of view and compare and contrast it with own point of view.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view and compare it to their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine whether the structure of a text enhances an author’s claim.
description_plain: Your child can decide if the way a text is organized helps support the author's main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words or phrases in a text, including words with multiple meanings and figurative language, impacts the meaning of the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain words or phrases, including those with more than one meaning or figurative language, affect the meaning of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify an event that occurred or an individual’s action in a short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can point out an event or action by a person in a short informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify one example that shows change in an individual, idea, or event over the course of a short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find an example that shows how a person, idea, or event changes in a short informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can determine one or more ways in which an individual, idea, or event changed over the course of an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain one or more ways a person, idea, or event changes in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how individuals, ideas, or events change over the course of the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how people, ideas, or events change throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the main idea of a short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in a short nonfiction text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the main idea of a short informational text, or identify one detail that supports the main idea (when the main idea is provided).
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in a short nonfiction text, or pick out one detail that supports the main idea if it is given.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the main idea of an informational text and one detail to support it.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in a nonfiction text and give one detail that supports it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central idea of a text; recount the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main idea of a text and retell what it says.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use details (pictures) to answer concrete who, what, where, or when questions about short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can use details or pictures to answer basic who, what, where, or when questions about a short nonfiction text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use concrete details to answer simple questions and make predictions about short informational text.
description_plain: Your child can use clear details to answer simple questions and make guesses about what will happen in a short nonfiction text.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use explicit and implicit information from an informational text to answer questions about the text.
description_plain: Your child can use information that is stated or suggested in a nonfiction text to answer questions about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a text to determine its meaning and cite textual evidence to support explicit and implicit understanding.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a text means and use examples from the text to support their answers, even if the answers are not directly stated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding while actively engaged in reading or listening to stories, dramas, and poems.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand stories, plays, and poems while reading or listening to them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate explicit understanding of recounted versions of foundational works of American literature.
description_plain: Your child can clearly understand and talk about retold versions of important American stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare two or more interpretations (e.g., recorded or live production of a play or recorded novel or poetry) of a story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more versions of a story, play, or poem, such as a live play and a recorded one.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the point of view when there is a difference between the author’s actual language and intended meaning.
description_plain: Your child can tell when the author’s words mean something different from what they seem to say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how the author’s choice of where to end the story contributes to the meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the place where the author ends the story helps give the story its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words or phrases in a text, including words with multiple meanings and figurative language, impact the meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain words or phrases, including those with more than one meaning or that use creative language, affect what the text means.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify an event that occurred or a character’s action in a short narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can point out something that happened or something a character did in a short story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify one example that shows change in a character, setting, or event from a short narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can find one example of how a character, setting, or event changes in a short story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can determine one or more ways in which a character, setting, and/or event changed or developed over the course of a narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can explain one or more ways a character, setting, or event changes or grows in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how characters, the setting or events change over the course of the story or drama.
description_plain: Your child can explain how characters, the setting, or events change as the story or play goes on.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the theme or main idea of a short narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme in a short story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can identify the theme or main idea of a short narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme in a short story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can determine how key events in a narrative text relate to the theme or main idea and/or help the reader infer it.
description_plain: Your child can explain how important events in a story connect to the main idea or theme, or help the reader figure it out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount the main events of the text which are related to the theme or central idea.
description_plain: Your child can retell the main events in a story that relate to its main idea or theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use details (pictures) to answer concrete who, what, where, or when questions about short narrative text.
description_plain: Your child can use details or pictures to answer basic who, what, where, or when questions about a short story.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use details from a narrative text to answer questions and make predictions.
description_plain: Your child can use details from a story to answer questions and make guesses about what might happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: The student can use explicit and implicit information from a narrative text to answer questions about the text.
description_plain: Your child can use information that is stated or suggested in a story to answer questions about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a text to determine its meaning and cite textual evidence to support explicit and implicit understandings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a story means and use examples from the story to support their answers, even if the answers are not directly stated.

subject: Math
description: The student can extend a simple AB pattern using pictures, objects or familiar symbols.
description_plain: Your child can continue a simple AB pattern using pictures, objects, or familiar symbols.

subject: Math
description: The student can extend a simple ABAB, ABC or ABBA pattern involving shapes, numbers or objects.
description_plain: Your child can continue a simple pattern like ABAB, ABC, or ABBA using shapes, numbers, or objects.

subject: Math
description: The student can create, describe and extend simple number patterns that involve a simple addition or subtraction rule.
description_plain: Your child can make, describe, and continue simple number patterns that use easy addition or subtraction rules.

subject: Math
description: Identify and extend numerical patterns.
description_plain: Your child can find and continue number patterns.

subject: Math
description: The student can identify the category in a bar graph or picture graph that has the most or least when the quantities have extreme differences.
description_plain: Your child can find which group in a bar graph or picture graph has the most or least when the differences are very clear.

subject: Math
description: The student can identify what type or quantity of data are needed to answer a question, solve a problem, or complete a chart or pictograph.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what kind or how much information is needed to answer a question, solve a problem, or fill in a chart or pictograph.

subject: Math
description: The student can read and interpret data from charts, tables, graphs, tallies and pictographs.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand information from charts, tables, graphs, tallies, and pictographs.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data on a picture, line plot, or bar graph.
description_plain: Your child can show and understand data using a picture, line plot, or bar graph.

subject: Math
description: The student can match coins of the same denomination (penny, nickel, dime, and quarter) when presented within a group.
description_plain: Your child can match coins of the same type, like pennies, nickels, dimes, or quarters, when looking at a group of coins.

subject: Math
description: The student can identify an equivalent value of a nickel, dime, and quarter.
description_plain: Your child can say how much a nickel, dime, or quarter is worth in another way.

subject: Math
description: The student can tell the amount of money using collections of coins up to and including $10.00.
description_plain: Your child can count coins to tell the total amount of money, up to $10.00.

subject: Math
description: Indicate relative value of collections of coins.
description_plain: Your child can tell which group of coins is worth more or less.

subject: Math
description: The student can compare the lengths or masses of two objects to determine which one is longer or heavier, where the difference is vastly different.
description_plain: Your child can compare two objects to see which one is longer or heavier when the difference is obvious.

subject: Math
description: The student can identify the appropriate tool for measuring length and weight.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right tool to measure how long or heavy something is.

subject: Math
description: The student can use an appropriate tool and customary units of measurement to measure length (inches/feet) and weight of objects (ounces/pounds).
description_plain: Your child can use the right tool to measure how long or heavy something is using inches, feet, ounces, or pounds.

subject: Math
description: Use standard units to measure weight and length of objects.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long or heavy things are using standard units.

subject: Math
description: The student can associate activities with morning and afternoon.
description_plain: Your child understands which activities happen in the morning and which happen in the afternoon.

subject: Math
description: The student can tell time to the hour using a digital clock or analog clock.
description_plain: Your child can tell what hour it is using a digital or regular clock.

subject: Math
description: The student can tell time to the quarter hour using a digital clock or to the half hour using an analog clock.
description_plain: Your child can tell time to the quarter hour on a digital clock or to the half hour on a regular clock.

subject: Math
description: Tell time using an analog or digital clock to the half or quarter hour.
description_plain: Your child can tell time to the half or quarter hour using a digital or regular clock.

subject: Math
description: The student can differentiate between empty and full.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between something that is empty and something that is full.

subject: Math
description: The student can demonstrate understanding that 3D shapes have volume.
description_plain: Your child understands that 3D shapes take up space and have volume.

subject: Math
description: The student can determine the volume of a rectangular prism by counting unit cubes.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how much space a box holds by counting small cubes that fit inside.

subject: Math
description: Determine the volume of a rectangular prism by counting units of measure (unit cubes).
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a box by counting how many unit cubes fit inside.

subject: Math
description: The student can differentiate between round and square or sphere and cube.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between round and square shapes, or between a ball and a box shape.

subject: Math
description: The student can match objects to their outlines.
description_plain: Your child can match objects to their outlines or shadows.

subject: Math
description: The student can identify a sphere, cube, cone, and cylinder.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name a sphere, cube, cone, and cylinder.

subject: Math
description: Identify common three-dimensional shapes.
description_plain: Your child can identify common 3D shapes like cubes, spheres, cones, and cylinders.

subject: Math
description: The student can identify two-dimensional shapes (circle, square, star).
description_plain: Your child can name flat shapes like circles, squares, and stars.

subject: Math
description: The student can identify two-dimensional figures with a common attribute.
description_plain: Your child can find flat shapes that have something in common, like the same number of sides.

subject: Math
description: The student can sort two-dimensional figures using attributes (e.g., angles, numbers of sides) they have in common.

description: Select and operate appropriate software to perform a variety of tasks, and recognize that users have different needs and preferences for the technology they use.

subject: Social Studies
description: Participate in projects to help or inform others.
description_plain: Your child will take part in projects that help or teach other people.

subject: Social Studies
description: Develop and implement an action plan to address or inform others about a classroom issue.
description_plain: Your child can make a simple plan and carry it out to help with or tell others about a classroom problem.

subject: Social Studies
description: Act constructively to further the public good.

subject: Social Studies
description: Express a position on a classroom issue.

subject: Social Studies
description: Communicate a reasoned position on a public issue.

subject: Social Studies

subject: Social Studies
description: Use simple graphs to explain information about a classroom issue.

subject: Social Studies

subject: Social Studies

description_plain: Your child can clearly say what a problem is, think about different points of view, and come up with possible solutions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Recognize situations in which people trade.

subject: Social Studies

description_plain: Your child understands basic ideas about how people buy, sell, and trade things in our economy.

description_plain: Your child can explain important rights and how people show responsibility by helping in their community.

description_plain: Your child understands that areas can be grouped together because they share things in common, like weather or buildings.

subject: Social Studies

subject: Social Studies
description: Use geographic representations to acquire, process, and report information from a spatial perspective.
description_plain: Your child can use maps, pictures, or models to find and share information about places.

subject: Social Studies

description_plain: Your child can talk about different ways to solve problems and guess what might happen if they try each way.

description_plain: Your child can think about different ways people can work together to share their ideas and make changes.

subject: Social Studies
description: Act out of the rule of law and hold others to the same standard.

description: Civic Participation

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe changes in the local community over time.Examples may include but are not limited to: types of businesses, architecture and landscape, jobs, transportation, population.
description_plain: Your child can describe how their community has changed over time, such as new businesses, buildings, or ways people get around.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how individuals and groups have made significant historical changes.
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain people or groups made important changes in history.

subject: Social Studies
description: Examine different perspectives of the same event in a community and explain how and why they are different.
description_plain: Your child can look at the same event from different people's points of view and explain why they might see it differently.

subject: Social Studies
description: Demonstrate chronological thinking by distinguishing among years and decades using a timeline of local community events.
description_plain: Your child can use a timeline to put community events in order and tell the difference between years and decades.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use historical thinking to understand the past.
description_plain: Your child can use clues and information to learn about and understand things that happened in the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use democratic procedures to make decisions on civic issues in the school or classroom.
description_plain: Your child can use fair and group decision-making methods, like voting, to help solve problems at school or in class.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain different strategies students and others could take to address problems and predict possible results.
description_plain: Your child can talk about different ways to solve problems and guess what might happen with each choice.

subject: Social Studies
description: Assess options for individuals and groups to plan and conduct activities intended to advance views on matters of public policy.
description_plain: Your child can look at different choices people or groups have to plan and do activities that support their opinions about community rules or laws.

subject: Social Studies
description: Act out of the rule of law and hold others to the same standard.
description_plain: Your child will follow rules and expect others to do the same.

subject: Social Studies
description: Civic Participation
description_plain: Your child will take part in activities that help their school or community.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the challenges people have faced and actions they have taken to address issues at different times and places.
description_plain: Your child can explain problems people have faced in different times and places, and what they did to solve them.

subject: Social Studies
description: Construct an argument and justify a decision supported with evidence.
description_plain: Your child can make a choice about an issue and explain why, using facts to support their answer.

subject: Social Studies
description: Apply Democratic Values or Constitutional Principles to support a position on an issue.
description_plain: Your child can use ideas like fairness and freedom to explain their opinion about a problem.

subject: Social Studies
description: State an issue as a question of public policy and discuss possible solutions from different perspectives.
description_plain: Your child can ask a question about a community rule or problem and talk about different ways to solve it.

subject: Social Studies
description: Public Discourse And Decision Making
description_plain: Your child will learn how to talk about problems and help make group decisions.

subject: Social Studies
